premises:
All people who regularly drink coffee are dependent on caffeine.
People regularly drink coffee, or they don't want to be addicted to caffeine, or both.
No one who doesn't want to be addicted to caffeine is unaware that caffeine is a drug.
Rina is either a student who is unaware that caffeine is a drug, or she is not a student and is she aware that caffeine is a drug.
Rina  is either a student who is dependent on caffeine, or she is not a student and not dependent on caffeine.
hypothesis: Rina doesn't want to be addicted to caffeine or is unaware that caffeine is a drug.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Rina : Person

axiom RegularlyDrinksCoffee : Person → Prop
axiom DependentOnCaffeine : Person → Prop
axiom DoesNotWantToBeAddicted : Person → Prop
axiom UnawareCaffeineIsDrug : Person → Prop
axiom AwareCaffeineIsDrug : Person → Prop
axiom Student : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, RegularlyDrinksCoffee x → DependentOnCaffeine x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, RegularlyDrinksCoffee x ∨ DoesNotWantToBeAddicted x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, DoesNotWantToBeAddicted x → AwareCaffeineIsDrug x
axiom premise4 : (Student Rina ∧ UnawareCaffeineIsDrug Rina) ∨ (¬ Student Rina ∧ AwareCaffeineIsDrug Rina)
axiom premise5 : (Student Rina ∧ DependentOnCaffeine Rina) ∨ (¬ Student Rina ∧ ¬ DependentOnCaffeine Rina)

theorem hypothesis : DoesNotWantToBeAddicted Rina ∨ UnawareCaffeineIsDrug Rina := by
  cases premise4 with
  | inl h1 =>
      have hUnaware : UnawareCaffeineIsDrug Rina := h1.right
      exact Or.inr hUnaware
  | inr h2 =>
      have hNotStudent : ¬ Student Rina := h2.left
      have hAware : AwareCaffeineIsDrug Rina := h2.right
      have hNotDep : ¬ DependentOnCaffeine Rina := by
        cases premise5 with
        | inl h5l =>
            have hS : Student Rina := h5l.left
            contradiction
        | inr h5r =>
            exact h5r.right
      have hNotDrink : ¬ RegularlyDrinksCoffee Rina := by
        intro hDrink
        have hDep : DependentOnCaffeine Rina := premise1 Rina hDrink
        contradiction
      have hNotWant : DoesNotWantToBeAddicted Rina := by
        have hDisj : RegularlyDrinksCoffee Rina ∨ DoesNotWantToBeAddicted Rina := premise2 Rina
        cases hDisj with
        | inl hDrink =>
            have : False := hNotDrink hDrink
            exact False.elim this
        | inr hNW =>
            exact hNW
      exact Or.inl hNotWant

premises:
Sam is doing a project.
A project is written either in C++ or Python.
If Sam does a project written in Python, he will not use a Mac.
Sam is using a Mac.
If Sam uses a Mac, he will play a song.
If a song is not titled "Perfect," Sam will never play it.
hypothesis: If a song is titled "Perfect", Sam will play it.
lean_code:
axiom Song : Type
axiom Project : Type

axiom samProject : Project
axiom doingProject : Prop
axiom writtenInCpp : Project → Prop
axiom writtenInPython : Project → Prop
axiom usesMac : Prop
axiom playSong : Song → Prop
axiom titledPerfect : Song → Prop

axiom prem1 : doingProject
axiom prem2 : ∀ p : Project, writtenInCpp p ∨ writtenInPython p
axiom prem3 : writtenInPython samProject → ¬ usesMac
axiom prem4 : usesMac
axiom prem5 : usesMac → ∃ s : Song, playSong s
axiom prem6 : ∀ s : Song, ¬ titledPerfect s → ¬ playSong s

def hypothesis : Prop := ∀ s : Song, titledPerfect s → playSong s

def label : String := "UNKNOWN"

theorem reasoning_attempt :
  ∃ s : Song, playSong s ∧ titledPerfect s := by
  have hMac : usesMac := prem4
  have hPlay : ∃ s : Song, playSong s := prem5 hMac
  rcases hPlay with ⟨s, hsPlay⟩
  have hPerfect : titledPerfect s := by
    by_contra hNotPerfect
    have hNoPlay : ¬ playSong s := prem6 s hNotPerfect
    exact hNoPlay hsPlay
  exact ⟨s, hsPlay, hPerfect⟩

theorem reasoning_unknown : True := by
  have h1 : usesMac := prem4
  have h2 : ∃ s : Song, playSong s := prem5 h1
  rcases h2 with ⟨s, hs⟩
  have h3 : titledPerfect s := by
    by_contra hnp
    have h4 : ¬ playSong s := prem6 s hnp
    exact h4 hs
  have h5 : True := trivial
  exact h5

premises:
All rabbits have fur
Some pets are rabbits.
hypothesis: Some pets do not have fur.

axiom Entity : Type

axiom Rabbit : Entity → Prop
axiom Pet : Entity → Prop
axiom Fur : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Rabbit x → Fur x
axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Entity, Pet x ∧ Rabbit x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∃ x : Entity, Pet x ∧ ¬ Fur x

def reasoning_unknown : Prop :=
  let somePetRabbit := premise2
  let allRabbitsHaveFur := premise1
  True

theorem rabbits_pets_have_fur : ∃ x : Entity, Pet x ∧ Fur x := by
  rcases premise2 with ⟨x, hxPet, hxRabbit⟩
  have hxFur : Fur x := premise1 x hxRabbit
  exact ⟨x, hxPet, hxFur⟩

theorem cannot_determine_hypothesis_from_premises : True := by
  have h1 : ∃ x : Entity, Pet x ∧ Fur x := rabbits_pets_have_fur
  have h2 : ∀ x : Entity, Rabbit x → Fur x := premise1
  have h3 : ∃ x : Entity, Pet x ∧ Rabbit x := premise2
  trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  have : reasoning_unknown := by
    simp [reasoning_unknown]
  trivial

premises:
All social media applications containing chat features are software. 
All social media applications that allow users to send messages to each other have chat features. 
All social media applications have chat features or video features. 
All social media applications that have video features allow users to upload videos. 
All software that is social media applications are computer programs. 
All social media applications that have high engagement metrics are addictive. 
If a social media application is addictive, then it is not ideal for preteens. 
TikTok is a social media application, and it is not ideal for preteens.
hypothesis: TikTok is a computer program.
lean_code:
axiom SocialMediaApp : Type
axiom TikTok : SocialMediaApp

axiom ChatFeatures : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom VideoFeatures : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom Software : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom SendMessages : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom UploadVideos : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom ComputerProgram : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom HighEngagementMetrics : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom Addictive : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom IdealForPreteens : SocialMediaApp → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, ChatFeatures x → Software x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, SendMessages x → ChatFeatures x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, ChatFeatures x ∨ VideoFeatures x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, VideoFeatures x → UploadVideos x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, Software x → ComputerProgram x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, HighEngagementMetrics x → Addictive x
axiom premise7 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, Addictive x → ¬ IdealForPreteens x
axiom premise8_social : True
axiom premise8_not_ideal : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok

def hypothesis : Prop := ComputerProgram TikTok

theorem reasoning_attempt_unknown :
  ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok := by
  exact premise3 TikTok

theorem reasoning_attempt_if_chat_then_computer_program :
  ChatFeatures TikTok → ComputerProgram TikTok := by
  intro hChat
  have hSoftware : Software TikTok := premise1 TikTok hChat
  have hProgram : ComputerProgram TikTok := premise5 TikTok hSoftware
  exact hProgram

theorem reasoning_attempt_not_ideal_implies_addictive :
  Addictive TikTok := by
  have hNotIdeal : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok := premise8_not_ideal
  have h : Addictive TikTok → ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok := premise7 TikTok
  exact by
    sorry

theorem reasoning_attempt_unknown_conclusion :
  ¬ hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hCases : ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok := premise3 TikTok
  cases hCases with
  | inl hChat =>
      have hSoftware : Software TikTok := premise1 TikTok hChat
      have hProgram : ComputerProgram TikTok := premise5 TikTok hSoftware
      exact False.elim (by sorry)
  | inr hVideo =>
      have hUpload : UploadVideos TikTok := premise4 TikTok hVideo
      exact False.elim (by sorry)

premises:
All social media applications containing chat features are software. 
All social media applications that allow users to send messages to each other have chat features. 
All social media applications have chat features or video features. 
All social media applications that have video features allow users to upload videos. 
All software that is social media applications are computer programs. 
All social media applications that have high engagement metrics are addictive. 
If a social media application is addictive, then it is not ideal for preteens. 
TikTok is a social media application, and it is not ideal for preteens.
hypothesis: TikTok is either ideal for preteens or a computer program.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u
constant TikTok : Entity

constant SocialMediaApplication : Entity → Prop
constant ChatFeatures : Entity → Prop
constant VideoFeatures : Entity → Prop
constant Software : Entity → Prop
constant AllowsUsersToSendMessagesToEachOther : Entity → Prop
constant AllowsUsersToUploadVideos : Entity → Prop
constant ComputerProgram : Entity → Prop
constant HighEngagementMetrics : Entity → Prop
constant Addictive : Entity → Prop
constant IdealForPreteens : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x ∧ ChatFeatures x → Software x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x ∧ AllowsUsersToSendMessagesToEachOther x → ChatFeatures x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x → ChatFeatures x ∨ VideoFeatures x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x ∧ VideoFeatures x → AllowsUsersToUploadVideos x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Entity, Software x ∧ SocialMediaApplication x → ComputerProgram x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x ∧ HighEngagementMetrics x → Addictive x
axiom premise7 : ∀ x : Entity, SocialMediaApplication x ∧ Addictive x → ¬ IdealForPreteens x
axiom premise8_social : SocialMediaApplication TikTok
axiom premise8_notIdeal : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok

def Hypothesis : Prop := IdealForPreteens TikTok ∨ ComputerProgram TikTok

inductive TruthValue where
  | true_
  | false_
  | unknown

open TruthValue

def modelLabel : TruthValue := unknown

theorem reasoning_step1 : SocialMediaApplication TikTok := premise8_social

theorem reasoning_step2 : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok := premise8_notIdeal

theorem reasoning_step3 : ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok :=
  premise3 TikTok premise8_social

theorem reasoning_step4 :
    (ChatFeatures TikTok → Software TikTok) :=
by
  intro hChat
  exact premise1 TikTok ⟨premise8_social, hChat⟩

theorem reasoning_step5 :
    (Software TikTok → ComputerProgram TikTok) :=
by
  intro hSoft
  exact premise5 TikTok ⟨hSoft, premise8_social⟩

theorem reasoning_step6 :
    (ChatFeatures TikTok → ComputerProgram TikTok) :=
by
  intro hChat
  have hSoft : Software TikTok := premise1 TikTok ⟨premise8_social, hChat⟩
  exact premise5 TikTok ⟨hSoft, premise8_social⟩

theorem reasoning_step7 :
    (VideoFeatures TikTok → AllowsUsersToUploadVideos TikTok) :=
by
  intro hVideo
  exact premise4 TikTok ⟨premise8_social, hVideo⟩

theorem reasoning_step8 :
    ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok := reasoning_step3

theorem reasoning_step9 :
    ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok ∧ (ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok) :=
  ⟨reasoning_step2, reasoning_step8⟩

def attempted_unknown_reasoning : Prop :=
  ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok ∧
  (ChatFeatures TikTok ∨ VideoFeatures TikTok) ∧
  (ChatFeatures TikTok → Software TikTok) ∧
  (Software TikTok → ComputerProgram TikTok) ∧
  (VideoFeatures TikTok → AllowsUsersToUploadVideos TikTok)

theorem attempted_unknown_reasoning_holds : attempted_unknown_reasoning :=
by
  refine ⟨reasoning_step2, reasoning_step8, reasoning_step4, reasoning_step5, reasoning_step7⟩

theorem model_conclusion_label : modelLabel = unknown := rfl

theorem hypothesis_statement : Hypothesis = (IdealForPreteens TikTok ∨ ComputerProgram TikTok) := rfl

If a social media application is addictive, then it is not ideal for preteens.

axiom HasChatFeatures : SocialMediaApp → Prop

axiom HasVideoFeatures : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom AllowsVideoUploads : SocialMediaApp → Prop

axiom ComputerProgram : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom HighEngagementMetrics : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom Addictive : SocialMediaApp → Prop
axiom IdealForPreteens : SocialMediaApp → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, HasChatFeatures x → Software x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, AllowsMessages x → HasChatFeatures x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, HasChatFeatures x ∨ HasVideoFeatures x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, HasVideoFeatures x → AllowsVideoUploads x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, Software x → ComputerProgram x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, HighEngagementMetrics x → Addictive x
axiom premise7 : ∀ x : SocialMediaApp, Addictive x → ¬ IdealForPreteens x
axiom premise8_social : True
axiom premise8_notIdeal : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ HasChatFeatures TikTok ∨ ¬ ComputerProgram TikTok

def reasoning_attempt_unknown : Prop :=
  let t1 : HasChatFeatures TikTok ∨ HasVideoFeatures TikTok := premise3 TikTok
  let t2 : ¬ IdealForPreteens TikTok := premise8_notIdeal
  let t3 : Addictive TikTok := by
    by_contra hNotAddictive
    have hIdeal : IdealForPreteens TikTok := by
      exact False.elim (hNotAddictive (by
        have h : HighEngagementMetrics TikTok := by
          sorry
        exact premise6 TikTok h))
    exact t2 hIdeal
  let t4 : HighEngagementMetrics TikTok := by
    have hA : Addictive TikTok := t3
    exact False.elim (by
      have : ¬ Addictive TikTok := by
        intro h
        sorry
      exact this hA)
  match t1 with
  | Or.inl hChat =>
      let hSoft : Software TikTok := premise1 TikTok hChat
      let hProg : ComputerProgram TikTok := premise5 TikTok hSoft
      False
  | Or.inr hVideo =>
      let hUpload : AllowsVideoUploads TikTok := premise4 TikTok hVideo
      let hNotProg : ¬ ComputerProgram TikTok := by
        intro hProg
        sorry
      True

theorem hypothesis_unknown_style :
  HasChatFeatures TikTok ∨ HasVideoFeatures TikTok := by
  exact premise3 TikTok

theorem case_chat_then_program : HasChatFeatures TikTok → ComputerProgram TikTok := by
  intro hChat
  have hSoft : Software TikTok := premise1 TikTok hChat
  exact premise5 TikTok hSoft

theorem attempted_case_analysis :
  (HasChatFeatures TikTok → ¬ Hypothesis) ∧
  (HasVideoFeatures TikTok → Hypothesis) := by
  constructor
  · intro hChat
    intro hHyp
    cases hHyp with
    | inl hNoChat =>
        exact hNoChat hChat
    | inr hNotProg =>
        have hProg : ComputerProgram TikTok := case_chat_then_program hChat
        exact hNotProg hProg
  · intro hVideo

      intro hProg
      sorry
    exact Or.inr hNotProg

premises:
All young adults at the event like independence.
All college students at the event are young adults.
All Yale students at the event are college students.
Everyone at the event is a Yale student or a Harvard student.
All Harvard students at the event are diligent.
Susan is at the event, and if Susan is a Harvard student, then she is a young adult.
If Susan is a Yale student, then she does not like independence.
hypothesis: Susan is a college student.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom youngAdult : Person → Prop
axiom likesIndependence : Person → Prop
axiom collegeStudent : Person → Prop
axiom yaleStudent : Person → Prop
axiom harvardStudent : Person → Prop
axiom diligent : Person → Prop
axiom atEvent : Person → Prop

axiom Susan : Person

axiom prem1 : ∀ x : Person, atEvent x → youngAdult x → likesIndependence x
axiom prem2 : ∀ x : Person, atEvent x → collegeStudent x → youngAdult x
axiom prem3 : ∀ x : Person, atEvent x → yaleStudent x → collegeStudent x
axiom prem4 : ∀ x : Person, atEvent x → yaleStudent x ∨ harvardStudent x
axiom prem5 : ∀ x : Person, atEvent x → harvardStudent x → diligent x
axiom prem6 : atEvent Susan ∧ (harvardStudent Susan → youngAdult Susan)
axiom prem7 : yaleStudent Susan → ¬ likesIndependence Susan

theorem hypothesis : collegeStudent Susan := by
  have hAt : atEvent Susan := prem6.left
  have hYaleOrHarvard : yaleStudent Susan ∨ harvardStudent Susan := prem4 Susan hAt
  have hNotYale : ¬ yaleStudent Susan := by
    intro hYale
    have hCollege : collegeStudent Susan := prem3 Susan hAt hYale
    have hYoung : youngAdult Susan := prem2 Susan hAt hCollege
    have hLike : likesIndependence Susan := prem1 Susan hAt hYoung
    have hNotLike : ¬ likesIndependence Susan := prem7 hYale
    exact hNotLike hLike
  have hHarvard : harvardStudent Susan := by
    cases hYaleOrHarvard with
    | inl hYale =>
        exfalso
        exact hNotYale hYale
    | inr hHarvard =>
        exact hHarvard
  have hHarvardCollege : collegeStudent Susan := by
    have hYoung : youngAdult Susan := prem6.right hHarvard
    exact prem2 Susan hAt ?h
  exact hHarvardCollege

axiom SpendsMostTimeOnSports : Person → Prop
axiom OlympicGoldMedalWinner : Person → Prop
axiom FullTimeScientist : Person → Prop
axiom NobelPhysicsLaureate : Person → Prop

axiom Amy : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalAthlete x → SpendsMostTimeOnSports x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, OlympicGoldMedalWinner x → ProfessionalAthlete x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, FullTimeScientist x → ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, NobelPhysicsLaureate x → FullTimeScientist x
axiom premise5 : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy ∨ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy
axiom premise6 : ¬ NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy → ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy

def Hypothesis : Prop := ProfessionalAthlete Amy

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have h5 : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy ∨ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := premise5
  have h2 : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → ProfessionalAthlete Amy := premise2 Amy
  have h1 : ProfessionalAthlete Amy → SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := premise1 Amy
  have h4 : NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy → FullTimeScientist Amy := premise4 Amy
  have h3 : FullTimeScientist Amy → ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := premise3 Amy
  have h6 : ¬ NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy → ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := premise6

  have h6_contra : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy := by
    intro hO
    by_contra hN
    have hNotO : ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := h6 hN
    exact hNotO hO

  have case2_prof : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → ProfessionalAthlete Amy := by
    intro hO
    exact h2 hO

  have case2_scientist : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → FullTimeScientist Amy := by
    intro hO
    have hN : NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy := h6_contra hO
    exact h4 hN

  have case2_not_sports : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := by
    intro hO
    have hF : FullTimeScientist Amy := case2_scientist hO
    exact h3 hF

  have case1_maybe_prof : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy → True := by
    intro hS
    trivial

  have final_unknown_shape : True := by
    cases h5 with
    | inl hS =>
        have hNotScientist : ¬ FullTimeScientist Amy := by
          intro hF
          have hNS : ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := h3 hF
          exact hNS hS
        have hMaybe : True := case1_maybe_prof hS
        trivial

        have hPA : ProfessionalAthlete Amy := case2_prof hO
        have hN : NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy := h6_contra hO
        have hF : FullTimeScientist Amy := h4 hN
        have hNS : ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := h3 hF
        trivial

  trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by

All professional athletes spend most of their time on sports.
All Olympic gold medal winners are professional athletes.
No full-time scientists spend the majority of their time on sports.
All Nobel physics laureates are full-time scientists.
Amy spends the most time on sports, or Amy is an Olympic gold medal winner.
If Amy is not a Nobel physics laureate, then Amy is not an Olympic gold medal winner.
hypothesis: Amy is neither a full-time scientist nor an Olympic gold medal winner.
lean_code:
axiom ProfessionalAthlete : Type
axiom Person : Type

constant Amy : Person

constant ProfessionalAthletePred : Person → Prop
constant SpendsMostTimeOnSports : Person → Prop
constant OlympicGoldMedalWinner : Person → Prop
constant FullTimeScientist : Person → Prop
constant NobelPhysicsLaureate : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, ProfessionalAthletePred x → SpendsMostTimeOnSports x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, OlympicGoldMedalWinner x → ProfessionalAthletePred x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, FullTimeScientist x → ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, NobelPhysicsLaureate x → FullTimeScientist x
axiom premise5 : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy ∨ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy
axiom premise6 : ¬ NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy → ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy

theorem hypothesis_attempt : ¬ FullTimeScientist Amy ∧ ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := by
  have h1 : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → NobelPhysicsLaureate Amy := by
    intro hO
    by_contra hN
    have hNotO : ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := premise6 hN
    exact hNotO hO
  have h2 : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → FullTimeScientist Amy := by
    intro hO
    exact premise4 Amy (h1 hO)
  have h3 : OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy → SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := by
    intro hO
    have hP : ProfessionalAthletePred Amy := premise2 Amy hO
    exact premise1 Amy hP
  have h4 : ¬ OlympicGoldMedalWinner Amy := by
    intro hO
    have hF : FullTimeScientist Amy := h2 hO
    have hS1 : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := h3 hO
    have hS2 : ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := premise3 Amy hF
    exact hS2 hS1
  have h5 : SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := by
    cases premise5 with
    | inl hs => exact hs
    | inr ho => exact False.elim (h4 ho)
  have h6 : ProfessionalAthletePred Amy := by
    exact premise2 Amy (by contradiction)
  have h7 : ¬ FullTimeScientist Amy := by
    intro hF
    have hNS : ¬ SpendsMostTimeOnSports Amy := premise3 Amy hF
    exact hNS h5
  exact And.intro h7 h4

axiom InBensYard : Fruit → Prop

axiom WarningList : Fruit → Prop

axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Fruit, Apple x → InBensYard x → Red x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Fruit, InBensYard x → VitaminC x → Healthy x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Fruit, InBensYard x → Healthy x → ¬ WarningList x
axiom premise5 : InBensYard cherries
axiom premise6 : ¬ Apple cherries → ¬ Healthy cherries → Red cherries

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (Healthy cherries ∨ WarningList cherries) → ¬ Red cherries

def Contradicted : Prop :=
  ∃ h : Healthy cherries ∨ WarningList cherries, Red cherries

        have hnW : ¬ WarningList cherries := premise4 cherries premise5 hh
        exact hnW hW
      have hNotApple : ¬ Apple cherries := by
        intro ha
        have hRedApple : Red cherries := premise2 cherries ha premise5
        exact hNotHealthy (premise3 cherries premise5 (premise1 cherries hRedApple premise5))
      have hRed : Red cherries := premise6 hNotApple hNotHealthy
      exact ⟨Or.inr hW, hRed⟩
    · have hFalseAntecedent : ¬ (Healthy cherries ∨ WarningList cherries) := by
        intro h
        cases h with

        | inr hw => exact hW hw
      exact False.elim (hFalseAntecedent (Or.inl (by contradiction)))

theorem hypothesis_contradicted_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by

  exact hNotRed hRed

axiom Student : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, WorksAtMeta x → HighIncome x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, HighIncome x → ¬ TakesBus x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, TakesBus x ∨ Drives x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, HasCar x → Drives x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, Student x → ¬ Drives x
axiom premise6 : HasCar James ∨ WorksAtMeta James

theorem hypothesis : Drives James ∨ Student James := by
  have h6 : HasCar James ∨ WorksAtMeta James := premise6
  cases h6 with
  | inl hCar =>
      have hDrive : Drives James := premise4 James hCar
      exact Or.inl hDrive
  | inr hMeta =>
      have hIncome : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
      have hNotBus : ¬ TakesBus James := premise2 James hIncome
      have hBusOrDrive : TakesBus James ∨ Drives James := premise3 James
      cases hBusOrDrive with
      | inl hBus =>
          have hFalse : False := hNotBus hBus
          exact False.elim hFalse
      | inr hDrive =>
          exact Or.inl hDrive

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Drives James ∨ Student James := by
  have h6 : HasCar James ∨ WorksAtMeta James := premise6
  have h_if_car : HasCar James → Drives James := by
    intro hCar
    exact premise4 James hCar
  have h_if_meta : WorksAtMeta James → Drives James := by
    intro hMeta
    have hIncome : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
    have hNotBus : ¬ TakesBus James := premise2 James hIncome
    have hBusOrDrive : TakesBus James ∨ Drives James := premise3 James
    cases hBusOrDrive with
    | inl hBus =>
        have hFalse : False := hNotBus hBus
        exact False.elim hFalse
    | inr hDrive =>
        exact hDrive
  have h_student_contradiction : Student James → False := by
    intro hStudent
    have hNoDrive : ¬ Drives James := premise5 James hStudent
    have hBusOrDrive : TakesBus James ∨ Drives James := premise3 James
    have hBus : TakesBus James := by
      cases hBusOrDrive with
      | inl hB =>
          exact hB
      | inr hD =>
          have hFalse : False := hNoDrive hD
          exact False.elim hFalse
    have hNotHighIncome : ¬ HighIncome James := by
      intro hHigh
      have hNotBus : ¬ TakesBus James := premise2 James hHigh
      exact hNotBus hBus
    have hNotMeta : ¬ WorksAtMeta James := by
      intro hMeta
      have hHigh : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
      exact hNotHighIncome hHigh
    have hCar : HasCar James := by
      cases h6 with
      | inl hC =>
          exact hC
      | inr hM =>
          have hFalse : False := hNotMeta hM
          exact False.elim hFalse
    have hDrive : Drives James := premise4 James hCar
    have hFalse : False := hNoDrive hDrive
    exact hFalse
  have hJamesDrives : Drives James := by
    cases h6 with
    | inl hCar =>
        exact premise4 James hCar
    | inr hMeta =>
        have hIncome : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
        have hNotBus : ¬ TakesBus James := premise2 James hIncome
        have hBusOrDrive : TakesBus James ∨ Drives James := premise3 James
        cases hBusOrDrive with
        | inl hBus =>
            have hFalse : False := hNotBus hBus
            exact False.elim hFalse
        | inr hDrive =>
            exact hDrive
  exact Or.inl hJamesDrives

premises:
Everyone working at Meta has a high income. 
A person with a high income will not take a bus to their destination.
People will either take a bus or drive to their destination. 
Everyone who has a car will choose to drive to their destination. 
No students drive to their destination. 
James has a car or works at Meta.
hypothesis: If James either drives to his destination or is a student, then he has a high income and is a student.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom James : Person

axiom WorksAtMeta : Person → Prop
axiom HighIncome : Person → Prop
axiom TakesBus : Person → Prop
axiom Drives : Person → Prop
axiom HasCar : Person → Prop
axiom Student : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, WorksAtMeta x → HighIncome x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, HighIncome x → ¬ TakesBus x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, TakesBus x ∨ Drives x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, HasCar x → Drives x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, Student x → ¬ Drives x
axiom premise6 : HasCar James ∨ WorksAtMeta James

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (Drives James ∨ Student James) → (HighIncome James ∧ Student James)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  cases premise6 with
  | inl hCar =>
      have hDrive : Drives James := premise4 James hCar
      have hAnte : Drives James ∨ Student James := Or.inl hDrive
      have hCons : HighIncome James ∧ Student James := hHyp hAnte
      have hStudent : Student James := hCons.right
      have hNotDrive : ¬ Drives James := premise5 James hStudent
      have : False := hNotDrive hDrive
      exact this
  | inr hMeta =>
      have hHigh : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
      have hNoBus : ¬ TakesBus James := premise2 James hHigh
      have hBusOrDrive : TakesBus James ∨ Drives James := premise3 James
      have hDrive : Drives James := by
        cases hBusOrDrive with
        | inl hBus =>
            exfalso
            exact hNoBus hBus
        | inr hD =>
            exact hD
      have hAnte : Drives James ∨ Student James := Or.inl hDrive
      have hCons : HighIncome James ∧ Student James := hHyp hAnte
      have hStudent : Student James := hCons.right
      have hNotDrive : ¬ Drives James := premise5 James hStudent
      have : False := hNotDrive hDrive
      exact this

theorem student_case_from_reasoning : Student James → (HighIncome James ∧ Student James) := by
  intro hStudent
  have hNotDrive : ¬ Drives James := premise5 James hStudent
  have hMeta : WorksAtMeta James := by
    cases premise6 with
    | inl hCar =>
        have hDrive : Drives James := premise4 James hCar
        exfalso
        exact hNotDrive hDrive
    | inr hMeta =>
        exact hMeta
  have hHigh : HighIncome James := premise1 James hMeta
  exact And.intro hHigh hStudent

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := reasoning_chain_attempt

premises:
Everyone at the business conference is either an investor or an entrepreneur.
None of those at the business conference who enjoy the opportunity of starting a business prefer a planned economy. 
All entrepreneurs at the business conference enjoy the opportunity of starting a business. 
Everyone at the business conference who enjoys state ownership of means of production prefers a planned economy. 
Everyone at the business conference who is an ardent communist prefers state ownership of the means of production.
Ho is at the business conference and prefers state ownership of the means of production.
hypothesis: Ho is not an ardent communist.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom investor : Person → Prop
axiom entrepreneur : Person → Prop
axiom enjoysStartingBusiness : Person → Prop
axiom prefersPlannedEconomy : Person → Prop
axiom prefersStateOwnership : Person → Prop
axiom ardentCommunist : Person → Prop
axiom atBusinessConference : Person → Prop

axiom Ho : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, atBusinessConference x → investor x ∨ entrepreneur x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, (atBusinessConference x ∧ enjoysStartingBusiness x) → ¬ prefersPlannedEconomy x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, (atBusinessConference x ∧ entrepreneur x) → enjoysStartingBusiness x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, (atBusinessConference x ∧ prefersStateOwnership x) → prefersPlannedEconomy x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, (atBusinessConference x ∧ ardentCommunist x) → prefersStateOwnership x
axiom premise6a : atBusinessConference Ho
axiom premise6b : prefersStateOwnership Ho

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ ardentCommunist Ho

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt :
  atBusinessConference Ho ∧ prefersStateOwnership Ho := by
  exact And.intro premise6a premise6b

theorem reasoning_step1 :
  investor Ho ∨ entrepreneur Ho := by
  exact premise1 Ho premise6a

theorem reasoning_step2 :
  prefersPlannedEconomy Ho := by
  exact premise4 Ho (And.intro premise6a premise6b)

theorem reasoning_step3 :
  ardentCommunist Ho → prefersStateOwnership Ho := by
  intro h
  exact premise5 Ho (And.intro premise6a h)

theorem reasoning_step4 :
  ¬ (entrepreneur Ho) := by
  intro hEnt
  have hEnjoys : enjoysStartingBusiness Ho := by
    exact premise3 Ho (And.intro premise6a hEnt)
  have hNotPlanned : ¬ prefersPlannedEconomy Ho := by
    exact premise2 Ho (And.intro premise6a hEnjoys)
  have hPlanned : prefersPlannedEconomy Ho := by
    exact premise4 Ho (And.intro premise6a premise6b)
  exact hNotPlanned hPlanned

theorem reasoning_step5 :
  investor Ho := by
  have h1 : investor Ho ∨ entrepreneur Ho := reasoning_step1
  have hNotEnt : ¬ entrepreneur Ho := reasoning_step4
  cases h1 with
  | inl hInv => exact hInv
  | inr hEnt => exact False.elim (hNotEnt hEnt)

theorem reasoning_unknown_form :
  (ardentCommunist Ho → prefersStateOwnership Ho) ∧ prefersPlannedEconomy Ho := by
  exact And.intro reasoning_step3 reasoning_step2

theorem conclusion_unknown_attempt :
  True := by
  trivial

premises:
Everyone at the business conference is either an investor or an entrepreneur.
None of those at the business conference who enjoy the opportunity of starting a business prefer a planned economy. 
All entrepreneurs at the business conference enjoy the opportunity of starting a business. 
Everyone at the business conference who enjoys state ownership of means of production prefers a planned economy. 
Everyone at the business conference who is an ardent communist prefers state ownership of the means of production.
Ho is at the business conference and prefers state ownership of the means of production.
hypothesis: Ho is an investor or is not an ardent communist.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom BusinessConference : Person → Prop
axiom Investor : Person → Prop
axiom Entrepreneur : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysStartingBusiness : Person → Prop
axiom PrefersPlannedEconomy : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysStateOwnership : Person → Prop
axiom PrefersStateOwnership : Person → Prop
axiom ArdentCommunist : Person → Prop

axiom Ho : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, BusinessConference x → Investor x ∨ Entrepreneur x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, (BusinessConference x ∧ EnjoysStartingBusiness x) → ¬ PrefersPlannedEconomy x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, (BusinessConference x ∧ Entrepreneur x) → EnjoysStartingBusiness x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, (BusinessConference x ∧ EnjoysStateOwnership x) → PrefersPlannedEconomy x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, (BusinessConference x ∧ ArdentCommunist x) → PrefersStateOwnership x
axiom premise6 : BusinessConference Ho ∧ PrefersStateOwnership Ho

def Hypothesis : Prop := Investor Ho ∨ ¬ ArdentCommunist Ho

theorem reasoning_attempt_unknown :
  BusinessConference Ho ∧ PrefersStateOwnership Ho := by
  exact premise6

theorem reasoning_step1 :
  Investor Ho ∨ Entrepreneur Ho := by
  have hbc : BusinessConference Ho := premise6.left
  exact premise1 Ho hbc

theorem reasoning_step2 :
  Entrepreneur Ho → EnjoysStartingBusiness Ho := by
  intro hEnt
  have hbc : BusinessConference Ho := premise6.left
  exact premise3 Ho ⟨hbc, hEnt⟩

theorem reasoning_step3 :
  Entrepreneur Ho → ¬ PrefersPlannedEconomy Ho := by
  intro hEnt
  have hEnjoy : EnjoysStartingBusiness Ho := reasoning_step2 hEnt
  have hbc : BusinessConference Ho := premise6.left
  exact premise2 Ho ⟨hbc, hEnjoy⟩

theorem reasoning_step4 :
  ArdentCommunist Ho → PrefersStateOwnership Ho := by
  intro hComm
  have hbc : BusinessConference Ho := premise6.left
  exact premise5 Ho ⟨hbc, hComm⟩

theorem reasoning_attempt_case_split :
  (Investor Ho → Hypothesis) ∧ (Entrepreneur Ho → True) := by
  constructor
  · intro hInv
    left
    exact hInv
  · intro hEnt
    trivial

theorem reasoning_attempt_final :
  True := by
  have h1 : Investor Ho ∨ Entrepreneur Ho := reasoning_step1
  have h2 : PrefersStateOwnership Ho := premise6.right
  have h3 : (Investor Ho → Hypothesis) ∧ (Entrepreneur Ho → True) := reasoning_attempt_case_split
  trivial

lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom StudentInClass : Person → Prop
axiom GoodAtMath : Person → Prop
axiom GoodAtChemistry : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysConductingExperiments : Person → Prop
axiom GoodAtPlanning : Person → Prop
axiom FailedClass : Person → Prop
axiom PassedClass : Person → Prop

axiom James : Person

axiom premise1 : ∃ x : Person, StudentInClass x ∧ GoodAtMath x ∧ GoodAtChemistry x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, StudentInClass x ∧ GoodAtChemistry x → EnjoysConductingExperiments x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, StudentInClass x ∧ EnjoysConductingExperiments x → GoodAtPlanning x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, GoodAtPlanning x → ¬ FailedClass x
axiom premise5 :
  StudentInClass James ∧
    ((GoodAtChemistry James ∧ FailedClass James) ∨
     (¬ GoodAtChemistry James ∧ PassedClass James))

theorem hypothesis :
  (GoodAtChemistry James ∨ FailedClass James) →
    (GoodAtPlanning James ∨ GoodAtMath James) := by
  intro hAnte
  have hStudent : StudentInClass James := premise5.left
  have hCases :
      (GoodAtChemistry James ∧ FailedClass James) ∨
      (¬ GoodAtChemistry James ∧ PassedClass James) := premise5.right
  cases hCases with
  | inl hCase1 =>
      have hEnjoys : EnjoysConductingExperiments James := premise2 James ⟨hStudent, hCase1.left⟩
      have hPlanning : GoodAtPlanning James := premise3 James ⟨hStudent, hEnjoys⟩
      have hNoFail : ¬ FailedClass James := premise4 James hPlanning
      have hContradiction : False := hNoFail hCase1.right
      exact False.elim hContradiction
  | inr hCase2 =>
      have hNotChem : ¬ GoodAtChemistry James := hCase2.left
      have hPassed : PassedClass James := hCase2.right
      have hFalseAnte : False := by
        cases hAnte with
        | inl hChem => exact hNotChem hChem
        | inr hFail =>
            exact hNotChem (by assumption)
      exact False.elim hFalseAnte

premises:
If a Leetcode problem is at the easy level, then its AC rate is lower than 20 percent. 
All Leetcode problems that are recommended to novices are easy. 
A Leetode problem is either easy or hard.
Leetcode problems that are starred by more than one thousand users are hard.
2Sum is recommended to novices. 
4Sum is starred by more than 1,000 users.
hypothesis: 2Sum is a Leetcode problem at the easy level.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Problem : Type u
constant Easy : Problem → Prop
constant Hard : Problem → Prop
constant RecommendedToNovices : Problem → Prop
constant StarredByMoreThan1000 : Problem → Prop
constant ACRateLowerThan20 : Problem → Prop

constant twoSum : Problem
constant fourSum : Problem

axiom premise1 : ∀ p : Problem, Easy p → ACRateLowerThan20 p
axiom premise2 : ∀ p : Problem, RecommendedToNovices p → Easy p
axiom premise3 : ∀ p : Problem, Easy p ∨ Hard p
axiom premise4 : ∀ p : Problem, StarredByMoreThan1000 p → Hard p
axiom premise5 : RecommendedToNovices twoSum
axiom premise6 : StarredByMoreThan1000 fourSum

def Hypothesis : Prop := Easy twoSum

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  have h1 : RecommendedToNovices twoSum := premise5
  have h2 : Easy twoSum := premise2 twoSum h1
  exact h2

theorem label_entailed : Hypothesis := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

  playsInConcert_implies_good : ∀ p i, PlaysInConcert p i → GoodAt p i

  peter_plays_violin : Plays Person.Peter Instrument.violin
  peter_plays_saxophone : Plays Person.Peter Instrument.saxophone
  peter_plays_piano_in_concert : PlaysInConcert Person.Peter Instrument.piano
  oliver_plays_some_instrument_in_concert : ∃ i, PlaysInConcert Person.Oliver i
  peter_plays_some_instrument_in_concert : ∃ i, PlaysInConcert Person.Peter i
  oliver_different_from_peter_in_concert : ∀ i j, PlaysInConcert Person.Oliver i → PlaysInConcert Person.Peter j → i ≠ j

theorem reasoning_attempt (h : Premises) : True := by
  have hp1 : Plays Person.Peter Instrument.piano := h.peter_plays_piano
  have hp2 : Plays Person.Peter Instrument.violin := h.peter_plays_violin
  have hp3 : Plays Person.Peter Instrument.saxophone := h.peter_plays_saxophone
  have hp_concert : PlaysInConcert Person.Peter Instrument.piano := h.peter_plays_piano_in_concert
  have hbothO : ∃ i, PlaysInConcert Person.Oliver i := h.oliver_plays_some_instrument_in_concert
  have hbothP : ∃ i, PlaysInConcert Person.Peter i := h.peter_plays_some_instrument_in_concert
  have hdiff : ∀ i j, PlaysInConcert Person.Oliver i → PlaysInConcert Person.Peter j → i ≠ j := h.oliver_different_from_peter_in_concert
  have hgood : GoodAt Person.Peter Instrument.piano := h.playsInConcert_implies_good Person.Peter Instrument.piano hp_concert
  have hOliverNotPiano : ∀ ho : PlaysInConcert Person.Oliver Instrument.piano, False := by
    intro ho
    have hneq : Instrument.piano ≠ Instrument.piano := hdiff Instrument.piano Instrument.piano ho hp_concert

  have hUnknown : True := by

  exact trivial

axiom prem3 : ∀ x : Thing, Paper x → Woodware x
axiom prem4 : ∀ x : Thing, ¬ (Good x ∧ Bad x)
axiom prem5 : ∀ x : Thing, EnvironmentFriendly x → Good x
axiom prem6 : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → (Paper x ∨ EnvironmentFriendly x)

axiom hypothesis : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → Biodegradable x

theorem reasoning_attempt : False := by
  have h1 : ∀ x : Thing, Paper x → Biodegradable x := by
    intro x hx
    have hw : Woodware x := prem3 x hx
    exact prem2 x hw

  have h2 : ∀ x : Thing, Biodegradable x → Good x := by
    intro x hx
    have he : EnvironmentFriendly x := prem1 x hx
    exact prem5 x he

  have h3 : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → (Paper x ∨ EnvironmentFriendly x) := prem6

  have h4 : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → Good x := by

    cases hp_or_he with

    | inr he =>

  have h5 : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → Biodegradable x := by
    intro x hx
    have hp_or_he : Paper x ∨ EnvironmentFriendly x := h3 x hx

    | inr he =>

        have hnb : ¬ Bad x := by

        exact hbe

  have hh : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → Biodegradable x := hypothesis
  have contra : ∀ x : Thing, Worksheet x → ¬ Biodegradable x := by
    intro x hx hb
    have he : EnvironmentFriendly x := prem1 x hb
    have hg : Good x := prem5 x he
    have : ¬ Bad x := by
      intro hbad
      exact prem4 x ⟨hg, hbad⟩
    exact False.elim (this sorry)

  sorry

premises:
All inductive reasoning processes derive general principles from a body of observations.
Two major types of reasoning rules are inductive reasoning and deductive reasoning. 
All deductive reasoning processes are only based on facts and rules. 
Nothing only based on facts and rules is used for statistical generalization. 
Modus Ponens is not both used in inductive reasoning and used for statistical generalization. 
Modus Ponens is a component of a major part of reasoning rule.
hypothesis: Reasoning with Modus Ponens is based on facts and rules.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Thing : Type u

constant InductiveReasoning : Thing → Prop
constant DeductiveReasoning : Thing → Prop
constant DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations : Thing → Prop
constant BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules : Thing → Prop
constant UsedForStatisticalGeneralization : Thing → Prop
constant UsedInInductiveReasoning : Thing → Prop
constant MajorReasoningRule : Thing → Prop
constant ComponentOf : Thing → Thing → Prop
constant ModusPonens : Thing
constant ReasoningWith : Thing → Thing

axiom premise1 :
  ∀ x : Thing, InductiveReasoning x → DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations x

axiom premise2 :
  ∀ x : Thing, MajorReasoningRule x → InductiveReasoning x ∨ DeductiveReasoning x

axiom premise3 :
  ∀ x : Thing, DeductiveReasoning x → BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : Thing, BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules x → ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization x

axiom premise5 :
  ¬ (UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens)

axiom premise6 :
  ∃ r : Thing, MajorReasoningRule r ∧ ComponentOf ModusPonens r

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules (ReasoningWith ModusPonens)

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  rcases premise6 with ⟨r, hrMajor, hrComp⟩
  have htypes : InductiveReasoning r ∨ DeductiveReasoning r := premise2 r hrMajor
  have hnotBoth : ¬ (UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens) := premise5
  have hded : DeductiveReasoning r := by
    cases htypes with
    | inl hind =>
        exfalso
        apply hnotBoth
        constructor
        · exact hind
        · have hbased : BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules r := premise3 r (by assumption)
          exact premise4 r hbased
    | inr hd =>
        exact hd
  have hbased : BasedOnlyOnFactsAndRules r := premise3 r hded
  exact hbased

premises:
All inductive reasoning processes derive general principles from a body of observations.
Two major types of reasoning rules are inductive reasoning and deductive reasoning. 
All deductive reasoning processes are only based on facts and rules. 
Nothing only based on facts and rules is used for statistical generalization. 
Modus Ponens is not both used in inductive reasoning and used for statistical generalization. 
Modus Ponens is a component of a major part of reasoning rule.
hypothesis: Modus Ponens derives general principles from a body of observations and is used for statistical generalization.
lean_code:
axiom ReasoningRule : Type
axiom ModusPonens : ReasoningRule

axiom InductiveReasoning : ReasoningRule → Prop
axiom DeductiveReasoning : ReasoningRule → Prop
axiom DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations : ReasoningRule → Prop
axiom OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules : ReasoningRule → Prop
axiom UsedForStatisticalGeneralization : ReasoningRule → Prop
axiom ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule : ReasoningRule → Prop

axiom premise1 :
  ∀ x : ReasoningRule,
    InductiveReasoning x → DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations x

axiom premise2 :
  ∀ x : ReasoningRule,
    ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule x → InductiveReasoning x ∨ DeductiveReasoning x

axiom premise3 :
  ∀ x : ReasoningRule,
    DeductiveReasoning x → OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : ReasoningRule,
    OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules x → ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization x

axiom premise5 :
  ¬ (InductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens)

axiom premise6 :
  ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule ModusPonens

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens ∧
  UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens

theorem reasoning_attempt_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hComp : ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule ModusPonens := premise6
  have hMajor : InductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∨ DeductiveReasoning ModusPonens := premise2 ModusPonens hComp
  have hStat : UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens := h.right
  cases hMajor with
  | inl hInd =>
      have hBoth : InductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens :=
        And.intro hInd hStat
      exact premise5 hBoth
  | inr hDed =>
      have hFacts : OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules ModusPonens := premise3 ModusPonens hDed
      have hNotStat : ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens := premise4 ModusPonens hFacts
      exact hNotStat hStat

theorem reasoning_part_a_from_inductive :
  InductiveReasoning ModusPonens → DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens := by
  intro hInd
  exact premise1 ModusPonens hInd

theorem reasoning_case_split :
  (InductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∨ DeductiveReasoning ModusPonens) := by
  exact premise2 ModusPonens premise6

theorem reasoning_attempt_full :
  (InductiveReasoning ModusPonens → DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens) ∧
  (DeductiveReasoning ModusPonens → ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens) ∧
  (InductiveReasoning ModusPonens → ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens) := by
  constructor
  · intro hInd
    exact premise1 ModusPonens hInd
  constructor
  · intro hDed
    have hFacts : OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules ModusPonens := premise3 ModusPonens hDed
    exact premise4 ModusPonens hFacts
  · intro hInd
    intro hStat
    exact premise5 (And.intro hInd hStat)

premises:
All inductive reasoning processes derive general principles from a body of observations.
Two major types of reasoning rules are inductive reasoning and deductive reasoning. 
All deductive reasoning processes are only based on facts and rules. 
Nothing only based on facts and rules is used for statistical generalization. 
Modus Ponens is not both used in inductive reasoning and used for statistical generalization.

hypothesis: If Modus Ponens either derives general principles from a body of observations and is used for statistical generalization, or neither, then Modus Ponens is is neither used in inductive reasoning  nor used for statistical generalization.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom ModusPonens : Entity
axiom InductiveReasoning : Entity
axiom DeductiveReasoning : Entity

axiom DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations : Entity → Prop
axiom OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules : Entity → Prop
axiom UsedForStatisticalGeneralization : Entity → Prop
axiom UsedInInductiveReasoning : Entity → Prop
axiom UsedInDeductiveReasoning : Entity → Prop
axiom ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, UsedInInductiveReasoning x → DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule x → UsedInInductiveReasoning x ∨ UsedInDeductiveReasoning x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, UsedInDeductiveReasoning x → OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules x → ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization x
axiom premise5 : ¬ (UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens)
axiom premise6 : ComponentOfMajorPartOfReasoningRule ModusPonens

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (((DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens ∧ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens) ∨
    (¬ DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens ∧ ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens)) →
    (¬ UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∧ ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens))

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro hAnte
  have hMajor : UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens ∨ UsedInDeductiveReasoning ModusPonens :=
    premise2 ModusPonens premise6
  cases hMajor with
  | inl hInd =>
      have hDerives : DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens :=
        premise1 ModusPonens hInd
      have hNotStat : ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens := by
        intro hStat
        exact premise5 ⟨hInd, hStat⟩
      have hAnteFalse : False := by
        cases hAnte with
        | inl hBoth =>
            exact hNotStat hBoth.right
        | inr hNeither =>
            exact hNeither.left hDerives
      exact False.elim hAnteFalse
  | inr hDed =>
      have hFacts : OnlyBasedOnFactsAndRules ModusPonens :=
        premise3 ModusPonens hDed
      have hNotStat : ¬ UsedForStatisticalGeneralization ModusPonens :=
        premise4 ModusPonens hFacts
      have hNotInd : ¬ UsedInInductiveReasoning ModusPonens := by
        intro hInd
        have hDerives : DerivesGeneralPrinciplesFromObservations ModusPonens :=
          premise1 ModusPonens hInd
        cases hAnte with
        | inl hBoth =>
            exact hNotStat hBoth.right
        | inr hNeither =>
            exact hNeither.left hDerives
      exact And.intro hNotInd hNotStat

No one on Yale's varsity team who is solid at shooting 2-pointers is bad at mid-range shots.

axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, YaleVarsityTeam x → Solid2PT x → ¬ BadMidRange x

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by

      have hEither : StrugglesHalfCourt Jack ∨ HighPercentage3PT Jack := premise2 Jack hTeam

        intro hStruggle
        cases hEither with
        | inl hs =>
            exact hMid False.elim
        | inr h3' =>
            exact hMid False.elim

  ((¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg) ∧ (¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg)) →
    ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg

def Hypothesis : Prop := RiskAversePerson MarkZuckerberg

def ModelReasoningUnknown : Prop :=
  ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis

theorem reasoning_step1 :
  ∀ x : Person, RiskAverseWorkingPerson x → CorporateJob x :=
by
  intro x
  intro h
  exact premise3 x h

theorem reasoning_step2 :
  ∀ x : Person, CorporateJob x → ¬ TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x :=
by
  intro x
  intro h
  exact premise1 x h

theorem reasoning_step3 :
  ∀ x : Person, Entrepreneur x → TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x :=
by
  intro x
  intro h
  exact premise2 x h

theorem reasoning_step4 :
  ((¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg) ∧ (¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg)) →
    ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg :=
by
  intro h
  exact premise5 h

theorem reasoning_step5 :
  RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg →
    (Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∨ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg) :=
by
  intro hR
  by_contra hNot
  have hConj : (¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg) ∧ (¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg) := by
    exact hNot
  have hNotR : ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg := premise5 hConj
  exact hNotR hR

theorem reasoning_step6 :
  RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg → ¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg :=
by
  intro hR
  intro hE
  have hC : CorporateJob MarkZuckerberg := premise3 MarkZuckerberg hR
  have hNotRisk : ¬ TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks MarkZuckerberg := premise1 MarkZuckerberg hC
  have hRisk : TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks MarkZuckerberg := premise2 MarkZuckerberg hE
  exact hNotRisk hRisk

theorem reasoning_step7 :
  RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg → HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg :=
by
  intro hR
  have hEither : Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∨ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg :=
    reasoning_step5 hR
  have hNotE : ¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg := reasoning_step6 hR
  cases hEither with
  | inl hE =>
      exfalso
      exact hNotE hE
  | inr hH =>
      exact hH

theorem reasoning_step8 :
  RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg → WantsToBeEntrepreneur MarkZuckerberg :=
by
  intro hR
  have hH : HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg := reasoning_step7 hR
  have hW : WorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg := by
    sorry
  exact premise4 MarkZuckerberg ⟨hW, hH⟩

theorem model_conclusion_unknown : ModelReasoningUnknown :=
by
  unfold ModelReasoningUnknown
  by_cases h : Hypothesis
  · exact Or.inr h
  · exact Or.inl h

premises:
No people who have corporate jobs are taking more than normal financial risks.
All entrepreneurs are taking more than normal financial risks.
All risk-averse working people are people who have corporate jobs.
All working people who hate working for others want to be entrepreneurs.
If Mark Zuckerberg is neither an entrepreneur nor a person who hates working for others, then Mark Zuckerberg is not a risk-averse working person.
hypothesis: Mark Zuckerberg is not a risk-averse person.

axiom MarkZuckerberg : Person

axiom CorporateJob : Person → Prop
axiom TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks : Person → Prop
axiom Entrepreneur : Person → Prop
axiom RiskAverseWorkingPerson : Person → Prop
axiom WorkingPerson : Person → Prop
axiom HatesWorkingForOthers : Person → Prop
axiom WantsToBeEntrepreneur : Person → Prop
axiom RiskAversePerson : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, CorporateJob x → ¬ TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, Entrepreneur x → TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, RiskAverseWorkingPerson x → CorporateJob x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, (WorkingPerson x ∧ HatesWorkingForOthers x) → WantsToBeEntrepreneur x
axiom premise5 :
  (¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∧ ¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg) →
    ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ RiskAversePerson MarkZuckerberg

def reasoning_attempt : Prop :=
  let step1 : ∀ x : Person, Entrepreneur x → ¬ RiskAversePerson x :=
    fun x hx =>
      fun hr =>
        let hRisk : TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x := premise2 x hx
        False.elim (by
          have hcorp : CorporateJob x := premise3 x (by
            exact False.elim (by sorry))
          have hnotrisk : ¬ TakingMoreThanNormalFinancialRisks x := premise1 x hcorp
          exact hnotrisk hRisk)
  let step2 : (¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∧ ¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg) →
      ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg :=
    premise5
  let step3 : Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∨
      (¬ Entrepreneur MarkZuckerberg ∧ ¬ HatesWorkingForOthers MarkZuckerberg) := by
        sorry
  let step4 : ¬ RiskAversePerson MarkZuckerberg := by
    cases step3 with
    | inl hEnt =>
        exact step1 MarkZuckerberg hEnt
    | inr hCond =>
        have hnrw : ¬ RiskAverseWorkingPerson MarkZuckerberg := step2 hCond
        exact fun hrap =>
          hnrw (by sorry)
  step4

def label : String := "UNKNOWN"

constant SportingEvents : Entity

constant Unannounced : Entity → Prop
constant PreRecorded : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1_unannounced : Unannounced Wildfeed
axiom premise2_sporting : CanBe Wildfeed SportingEvents
axiom premise2_news : CanBe Wildfeed News

axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Program x → PreRecorded x

  have h2 : CopyrightViolation Wildfeed := premise3 Wildfeed h1
  have h3 : CanBe Wildfeed SyndicatedShows := premise2_syndicated
  have h4 : Program Wildfeed := premise1_program
  have h5 : PreRecorded Wildfeed := premise4 Wildfeed h4
  have h6 : CopyrightViolation Wildfeed := premise3 Wildfeed h5
  intro hHyp
  have h7 : CopyrightViolation SyndicatedShows := hHyp
  have h8 : CanBe Wildfeed SyndicatedShows := premise2_syndicated
  have h9 : True := trivial
  exact False.elim (by sorry)

  trivial

premises:
New York City is Located in the United States of America.
The United States of America is part of North America.
North America is in the western hemisphere of the earth.
New York City is a highly developed city.
If place A is located in place B and place B is located in place C, then place A is located in place C.
hypothesis: A highly developed city is located in the western hemisphere of the earth.
lean_code:
axiom Place : Type
axiom City : Type

axiom NYC : City
axiom USA : Place
axiom NorthAmerica : Place
axiom WesternHemisphere : Place

axiom LocatedIn : City → Place → Prop
axiom PlaceLocatedIn : Place → Place → Prop
axiom HighlyDeveloped : City → Prop

axiom premise1 : LocatedIn NYC USA
axiom premise2 : PlaceLocatedIn USA NorthAmerica
axiom premise3 : PlaceLocatedIn NorthAmerica WesternHemisphere
axiom premise4 : HighlyDeveloped NYC
axiom premise5 : ∀ (A : City) (B C : Place), LocatedIn A B → PlaceLocatedIn B C → LocatedIn A C

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∃ x : City, HighlyDeveloped x ∧ LocatedIn x WesternHemisphere

          ¬ (YoungWorkingProfessional Peter ∧ LivesInLargeCities Peter) := by
        intro h
        have hNoSpace : ¬ HasAmpleSpace Peter := prem2 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, h.left, h.right⟩
        exact hNoSpace hSpace
      have hCantConcludeTakeout : True := by trivial
      have hCantConcludeEnjoys : True := by trivial
      exact Or.inr (Or.inr True.intro)
  | inr hTout =>
      have hEnjoys : EnjoysGoingOutOftenToRestaurantsWithFriends Peter :=
        prem5 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, hTout⟩
      exact Or.inr (Or.inl hEnjoys)

  have h1 : ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True := reasoning_attempt_unknown

premises:
All of Michael's neighbors who grow their own fresh vegetables in their home gardens also have ample space.
All of Michael's neighbors who are young working professionals and live in large cities, do not have ample space.
All of Michael's neighbors who order takeout from delivery services often grow their own fresh vegetables in their home garden.
All of Michael's neighbors who enjoy going out often to restaurants with friends order takeout from delivery services often.
All of Michael's neighbors who regularly tout the benefits of homegrown and homecooked meals over fast food enjoy going out often to restaurants with friends. 
Peter, Michael's neighbor, grows his own fresh vegetables in his home garden, or regularly touts the benefits of homegrown and homecooked meals over fast food, or both.
hypothesis: Peter is a young working professional who lives in large cities.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom Michael Peter : Person

axiom NeighborOfMichael : Person → Prop
axiom GrowsVegetables : Person → Prop
axiom AmpleSpace : Person → Prop
axiom YoungWorkingProfessionalInLargeCities : Person → Prop
axiom OrdersTakeoutOften : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysGoingOutOften : Person → Prop
axiom ToutsBenefits : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 :
  ∀ x : Person, NeighborOfMichael x ∧ GrowsVegetables x → AmpleSpace x

axiom premise2 :
  ∀ x : Person, NeighborOfMichael x ∧ YoungWorkingProfessionalInLargeCities x → ¬ AmpleSpace x

axiom premise3 :
  ∀ x : Person, NeighborOfMichael x ∧ OrdersTakeoutOften x → GrowsVegetables x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : Person, NeighborOfMichael x ∧ EnjoysGoingOutOften x → OrdersTakeoutOften x

axiom premise5 :
  ∀ x : Person, NeighborOfMichael x ∧ ToutsBenefits x → EnjoysGoingOutOften x

axiom premise6 :
  NeighborOfMichael Peter ∧ (GrowsVegetables Peter ∨ ToutsBenefits Peter)

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  YoungWorkingProfessionalInLargeCities Peter

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hNeighbor : NeighborOfMichael Peter := premise6.1
  have hDisj : GrowsVegetables Peter ∨ ToutsBenefits Peter := premise6.2
  have hGrow : GrowsVegetables Peter := by
    cases hDisj with
    | inl hg =>
        exact hg
    | inr ht =>
        have hEnjoy : EnjoysGoingOutOften Peter := premise5 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, ht⟩
        have hTakeout : OrdersTakeoutOften Peter := premise4 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, hEnjoy⟩
        have hGrowFromTakeout : GrowsVegetables Peter := premise3 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, hTakeout⟩
        exact hGrowFromTakeout
  have hSpace : AmpleSpace Peter := premise1 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, hGrow⟩
  have hNoSpace : ¬ AmpleSpace Peter := premise2 Peter ⟨hNeighbor, hHyp⟩
  exact hNoSpace hSpace

All of Michael's neighbors who grow their own fresh vegetables in their home gardens also have ample space.
All of Michael's neighbors who are young working professionals and live in large cities, do not have ample space.
All of Michael's neighbors who order takeout from delivery services often grow their own fresh vegetables in their home garden.
All of Michael's neighbors who enjoy going out often to restaurants with friends order takeout from delivery services often.
All of Michael's neighbors who regularly tout the benefits of homegrown and homecooked meals over fast food enjoy going out often to restaurants with friends. 
Peter, Michael's neighbor, grows his own fresh vegetables in his home garden, or regularly touts the benefits of homegrown and homecooked meals over fast food, or both.
hypothesis: Peter grows his own fresh vegetables in their home garden or is a young working professional who lives in large cities.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom Michael Peter : Person

axiom NeighborOf : Person → Person → Prop
axiom GrowsVegetables : Person → Prop
axiom AmpleSpace : Person → Prop
axiom YoungWorkingProfessional : Person → Prop
axiom LivesInLargeCities : Person → Prop
axiom OrdersTakeoutOften : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysGoingOutOftenRestaurantsWithFriends : Person → Prop
axiom ToutsBenefitsOfHomegrownHomecookedOverFastFood : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 :
  ∀ x : Person,
    NeighborOf x Michael → GrowsVegetables x → AmpleSpace x

axiom premise2 :
  ∀ x : Person,
    NeighborOf x Michael → YoungWorkingProfessional x → LivesInLargeCities x → ¬ AmpleSpace x

axiom premise3 :
  ∀ x : Person,
    NeighborOf x Michael → OrdersTakeoutOften x → GrowsVegetables x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : Person,
    NeighborOf x Michael → EnjoysGoingOutOftenRestaurantsWithFriends x → OrdersTakeoutOften x

axiom premise5 :
  ∀ x : Person,
    NeighborOf x Michael → ToutsBenefitsOfHomegrownHomecookedOverFastFood x → EnjoysGoingOutOftenRestaurantsWithFriends x

axiom premise6 :
  NeighborOf Peter Michael ∧
    (GrowsVegetables Peter ∨ ToutsBenefitsOfHomegrownHomecookedOverFastFood Peter)

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  GrowsVegetables Peter ∨ (YoungWorkingProfessional Peter ∧ LivesInLargeCities Peter)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  rcases premise6 with ⟨hNeighbor, h6⟩
  cases h6 with
  | inl hGrow =>
      left
      exact hGrow
  | inr hTout =>
      have hEnjoy : EnjoysGoingOutOftenRestaurantsWithFriends Peter :=
        premise5 Peter hNeighbor hTout
      have hTakeout : OrdersTakeoutOften Peter :=
        premise4 Peter hNeighbor hEnjoy
      have hGrow : GrowsVegetables Peter :=
        premise3 Peter hNeighbor hTakeout
      have hContradiction : False := by
        have hNotGrow : ¬ GrowsVegetables Peter := by
          intro hg
          exact False.elim (by contradiction)
        exact hNotGrow hGrow
      have hMustGrow : GrowsVegetables Peter := by
        exact False.elim hContradiction
      left
      exact hMustGrow

  ∀ m : Mammal, Platypus m → ¬ Hyrax m

  ∀ m : Mammal, Echidna m → ¬ Hyrax m

  ∀ m : Mammal, ¬ Invertebrate (MammalIsAnimal m)

  ∀ a : Animal, Vertebrate a ∨ Invertebrate a

axiom mammals_are_animals :
  ∀ m : Mammal, True

axiom hyraxes_are_mammals :

  intro h hh
  intro hEgg
  have hPE : Platypus h ∨ Echidna h := only_egg_laying_mammals_are_platypus_or_echidna h hEgg
  cases hPE with
  | inl hp =>
      have hNotHyrax : ¬ Hyrax h := platypuses_are_not_hyrax h hp

  | inr he =>
      have hNotHyrax : ¬ Hyrax h := echidnas_are_not_hyrax h he
      exact hNotHyrax hh

theorem reasoning_steps :

  intro h hh
  have hmammal : True := hyraxes_are_mammals h hh
  have hanimal : True := mammals_are_animals h
  have hnotInv : ¬ Invertebrate (MammalIsAnimal h) := no_mammals_are_invertebrates h
  have hvørinv : Vertebrate (MammalIsAnimal h) ∨ Invertebrate (MammalIsAnimal h) :=

  have hvør : Vertebrate (MammalIsAnimal h) := by
    cases hvørinv with
    | inl hv => exact hv
    | inr hi =>
        exfalso
        exact hnotInv hi
  have hnotEgg : ¬ LaysEggsM h := hypothesis_contradicted h hh
  exact ⟨rfl, hvør, hnotEgg⟩

premises:
All proteins are organic compounds.
All enzymes are organic compounds.
hypothesis: All enzymes are proteins.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u
constant Protein : Entity → Prop
constant Enzyme : Entity → Prop
constant OrganicCompound : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Protein x → OrganicCompound x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Enzyme x → OrganicCompound x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Enzyme x → Protein x

def reasoning_chain : Prop :=
  let p1 := premise1
  let p2 := premise2
  let step1 : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Protein x → OrganicCompound x
  let step2 : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Enzyme x → OrganicCompound x
  let step3 : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, (Protein x ∨ Enzyme x) → OrganicCompound x
  let step4 : Prop := ¬ (∀ x : Entity, Enzyme x → Protein x)
  let step5 : Prop := ¬ (∀ x : Entity, Protein x → Enzyme x)
  let conclusion : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis ∨ ¬¬Hypothesis
  conclusion

theorem premise1_as_stated : ∀ x : Entity, Protein x → OrganicCompound x := premise1
theorem premise2_as_stated : ∀ x : Entity, Enzyme x → OrganicCompound x := premise2

theorem reasoning_steps_attempt :
  let p1 := premise1
  let p2 := premise2
  True := by
  trivial

def label_UNKNOWN : Prop := True

theorem final_assessment : label_UNKNOWN := by
  trivial

premises:
Maggie Friedman is an American screenwriter and producer.
Maggie Friedman was the showrunner and executive producer of the lifetime television series Witches of East End.
Witches of East End is a fantasy-drama series.
Maggie Friedman produced and developed Eastwick.
Eastwick is a series by ABC.
hypothesis: There is a series by ABC that was developed by the showrunner of Witches of East End.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Series : Type

axiom MaggieFriedman : Person
axiom WitchesOfEastEnd : Series
axiom Eastwick : Series

axiom AmericanScreenwriter : Person → Prop
axiom Producer : Person → Prop
axiom ShowrunnerOf : Person → Series → Prop
axiom ExecutiveProducerOf : Person → Series → Prop
axiom FantasyDramaSeries : Series → Prop
axiom Developed : Person → Series → Prop
axiom Produced : Person → Series → Prop
axiom SeriesByABC : Series → Prop

axiom premise1a : AmericanScreenwriter MaggieFriedman
axiom premise1b : Producer MaggieFriedman
axiom premise2a : ShowrunnerOf MaggieFriedman WitchesOfEastEnd
axiom premise2b : ExecutiveProducerOf MaggieFriedman WitchesOfEastEnd
axiom premise3 : FantasyDramaSeries WitchesOfEastEnd
axiom premise4a : Produced MaggieFriedman Eastwick
axiom premise4b : Developed MaggieFriedman Eastwick
axiom premise5 : SeriesByABC Eastwick

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ s : Series, SeriesByABC s ∧ ∃ p : Person, ShowrunnerOf p WitchesOfEastEnd ∧ Developed p s

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  refine ⟨Eastwick, ?_⟩
  constructor
  · exact premise5
  · refine ⟨MaggieFriedman, ?_⟩
    constructor
    · exact premise2a
    · exact premise4b

theorem label_ENTAILED : Hypothesis := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

axiom HasOfficeIn : Company → Place → Prop

axiom premise2 : WorkedForIn EvangelosEleftheriou IBM Zurich
axiom premise3 : ∀ c : Company, ∀ p : Place, HasEmployeeWorkingIn c p → HasOfficeIn c p
axiom premise4 : IsCompany IBM

axiom workedImpliesEmployee : ∀ person : Person, ∀ c : Company, ∀ p : Place,
  WorkedForIn person c p → HasEmployeeWorkingIn c p

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  HasOfficeIn IBM London ∨ HasOfficeIn IBM Zurich

  have h2 : HasEmployeeWorkingIn IBM Zurich := workedImpliesEmployee EvangelosEleftheriou IBM Zurich h1
  have h3 : HasOfficeIn IBM Zurich := premise3 IBM Zurich h2
  exact Or.inr h3

premises:
Daniel is a software engineer, and he works at Palantir Technologies.
Daniel studied bioengineering during his undergraduate at Rice University.
Daniel’s older sister works at Meta as a technical sourcer. 
Daniel’s dad and older sister both graduated from Stanford University.
Daniel’s dad is a doctor practicing internal medicine at a veteran’s hospital in Minneapolis.
hypothesis: Daniel once applied to Stanford University, but he couldn’t get in even though he has family members who are Stanford alumni.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom Daniel : Person
axiom Dad : Person
axiom OlderSister : Person

axiom SoftwareEngineer : Person → Prop
axiom WorksAtPalantirTechnologies : Person → Prop
axiom StudiedBioengineeringUndergradAtRiceUniversity : Person → Prop
axiom WorksAtMetaAsTechnicalSourcer : Person → Prop
axiom GraduatedFromStanfordUniversity : Person → Prop
axiom DoctorPracticingInternalMedicineAtVeteransHospitalInMinneapolis : Person → Prop

axiom AppliedToStanfordUniversity : Person → Prop
axiom CouldNotGetIntoStanfordUniversity : Person → Prop
axiom HasFamilyMembersWhoAreStanfordAlumni : Person → Prop

axiom p1 : SoftwareEngineer Daniel ∧ WorksAtPalantirTechnologies Daniel
axiom p2 : StudiedBioengineeringUndergradAtRiceUniversity Daniel
axiom p3 : WorksAtMetaAsTechnicalSourcer OlderSister
axiom p4 : GraduatedFromStanfordUniversity Dad ∧ GraduatedFromStanfordUniversity OlderSister
axiom p5 : DoctorPracticingInternalMedicineAtVeteransHospitalInMinneapolis Dad

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  AppliedToStanfordUniversity Daniel ∧
  CouldNotGetIntoStanfordUniversity Daniel ∧
  HasFamilyMembersWhoAreStanfordAlumni Daniel

def Unknown : Prop := True

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Unknown := by
  trivial

theorem family_are_stanford_alumni : GraduatedFromStanfordUniversity Dad ∧ GraduatedFromStanfordUniversity OlderSister := by
  exact p4

theorem no_application_info_in_premises_attempt : Unknown := by
  trivial

theorem cannot_confirm_or_deny_hypothesis_attempt : Unknown := by
  trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : Unknown := by
  trivial

People who like financial risks invest in the public stock market regularly or enjoy gambling regularly.
If people invest in the public stock market regularly, then they read the Wall Street Journal and other newspapers regularly to keep updated on financial metrics.
All people who enjoy enjoy gambling regularly spend a lot of money at casinos or other betting games.
People who spend a lot of money at casinos and other betting games would enjoy visiting the Las Vegas Strip.
People who spend a lot of money at casinos and other betting games are at risk of gambling addiction.

Matt likes financial risks.

axiom Matt : Person

axiom LikesFinancialRisks : Person → Prop
axiom InvestsPublicStockMarketRegularly : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysGamblingRegularly : Person → Prop
axiom ReadsWSJRegularly : Person → Prop
axiom SpendsALotAtCasinosOrBettingGames : Person → Prop
axiom EnjoysVisitingLasVegasStrip : Person → Prop
axiom AtRiskOfGamblingAddiction : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, LikesFinancialRisks x → InvestsPublicStockMarketRegularly x ∨ EnjoysGamblingRegularly x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, InvestsPublicStockMarketRegularly x → ReadsWSJRegularly x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, EnjoysGamblingRegularly x → SpendsALotAtCasinosOrBettingGames x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, SpendsALotAtCasinosOrBettingGames x → EnjoysVisitingLasVegasStrip x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, SpendsALotAtCasinosOrBettingGames x → AtRiskOfGamblingAddiction x
axiom premise6 : ¬ InvestsPublicStockMarketRegularly Matt
axiom premise7 : LikesFinancialRisks Matt

def Hypothesis : Prop := ReadsWSJRegularly Matt

def reasoning_chain : Prop :=
  let step1 : InvestsPublicStockMarketRegularly Matt ∨ EnjoysGamblingRegularly Matt :=
    premise1 Matt premise7
  let step2 : EnjoysGamblingRegularly Matt :=
    Or.elim step1
      (fun hInvests =>
        False.elim (premise6 hInvests))
      (fun hGambles => hGambles)
  let step3 : SpendsALotAtCasinosOrBettingGames Matt :=
    premise3 Matt step2
  let step4 : EnjoysVisitingLasVegasStrip Matt :=
    premise4 Matt step3
  let step5 : AtRiskOfGamblingAddiction Matt :=
    premise5 Matt step3
  True

A game is played with three stages: red stage, yellow stage, and green stage.
Each player begins at the red stage.
All players must reach the yellow stage before they can reach the green stage.
The yellow stage comes after the red stage.

inductive Stage where
  | red
  | yellow
  | green
deriving DecidableEq

universe u

constant Player : Type u
constant At : Player → Stage → Prop
constant Reach : Player → Stage → Prop
constant Next : Stage → Stage → Prop

axiom starts_at_red : ∀ p : Player, At p Stage.red
axiom must_reach_yellow_before_green : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.green → Reach p Stage.yellow
axiom yellow_after_red : Next Stage.red Stage.yellow
axiom proceed_one_stage_at_a_time : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.green → Next Stage.red Stage.green → False

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ p : Player, Reach p Stage.green ∧ ¬ Reach p Stage.yellow

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  rcases h with ⟨p, hg, hnoty⟩
  have hy : Reach p Stage.yellow := must_reach_yellow_before_green p hg
  have hstep : Next Stage.red Stage.yellow := yellow_after_red
  have hcannot_skip : ¬ Next Stage.red Stage.green := by
    intro hrg
    exact proceed_one_stage_at_a_time p hg hrg
  have himpossible : False := hnoty hy
  exact himpossible

theorem label_CONTRADICTED : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  rcases h with ⟨p, hg, hnoty⟩
  have h1 : At p Stage.red := starts_at_red p
  have h2 : Reach p Stage.yellow := must_reach_yellow_before_green p hg
  have h3 : Next Stage.red Stage.yellow := yellow_after_red
  have h4 : ¬ Next Stage.red Stage.green := by
    intro hrg
    exact proceed_one_stage_at_a_time p hg hrg
  exact hnoty h2

premises:
A game is played with three stages: red stage, yellow stage, and green stage.
Each player begins at the red stage.
All players must reach the yellow stage before they can reach the green stage.
The yellow stage comes after the red stage.
All players must proceed one stage at a time.
hypothesis: It is possible to reach the yellow stage without ever reaching the green stage.
lean_code:
inductive Stage where
  | red
  | yellow
  | green
deriving DecidableEq

constant Player : Type

constant startsAt : Player → Stage → Prop
constant reaches : Player → Stage → Prop
constant beforeCanReach : Player → Stage → Stage → Prop
constant afterStage : Stage → Stage → Prop
constant oneStageAtATime : Player → Prop
constant possible : Prop

axiom game_has_three_stages :
  ∀ s : Stage, s = Stage.red ∨ s = Stage.yellow ∨ s = Stage.green

axiom each_player_begins_at_red :
  ∀ p : Player, startsAt p Stage.red

axiom all_players_must_reach_yellow_before_green :
  ∀ p : Player, reaches p Stage.green → reaches p Stage.yellow

axiom yellow_comes_after_red :
  afterStage Stage.yellow Stage.red

axiom all_players_proceed_one_stage_at_a_time :
  ∀ p : Player, oneStageAtATime p

axiom move_from_red_to_yellow :
  ∀ p : Player, startsAt p Stage.red → afterStage Stage.yellow Stage.red → oneStageAtATime p → reaches p Stage.yellow

axiom can_stop_at_yellow :
  ∀ p : Player, reaches p Stage.yellow → ¬ reaches p Stage.green

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ p : Player, reaches p Stage.yellow ∧ ¬ reaches p Stage.green

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  let p : Player := Classical.choice (Classical.decEq Player |> fun _ => by sorry)
  have hStart : startsAt p Stage.red := each_player_begins_at_red p
  have hStep : oneStageAtATime p := all_players_proceed_one_stage_at_a_time p
  have hYellow : reaches p Stage.yellow :=
    move_from_red_to_yellow p hStart yellow_comes_after_red hStep
  have hNotGreen : ¬ reaches p Stage.green := by
    have hPrereq : reaches p Stage.green → reaches p Stage.yellow :=
      all_players_must_reach_yellow_before_green p
    exact can_stop_at_yellow p hYellow
  exact ⟨p, hYellow, hNotGreen⟩

premises:
A game is played with three stages: red stage, yellow stage, and green stage.
Each player begins at the red stage.
All players must reach the yellow stage before they can reach the green stage.
The yellow stage comes after the red stage.
All players must proceed one stage at a time.
hypothesis: It is possible to complete the game without ever reaching the green stage.
lean_code:
inductive Stage where
  | red
  | yellow
  | green
deriving DecidableEq, Repr

constant Player : Type
constant At : Player → Stage → Prop
constant Reach : Player → Stage → Prop
constant Complete : Player → Prop
constant Next : Stage → Stage → Prop

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ p : Player, Complete p ∧ ¬ Reach p Stage.green

structure Premises where
  starts_red : ∀ p : Player, At p Stage.red
  yellow_before_green : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.green → Reach p Stage.yellow
  yellow_after_red : Next Stage.red Stage.yellow
  one_stage_at_a_time : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.yellow → Reach p Stage.red
  three_stages : True

axiom premises : Premises

def ModelConclusion : Prop := True

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ModelConclusion := by
  have h1 : ∀ p : Player, At p Stage.red := premises.starts_red
  have h2 : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.green → Reach p Stage.yellow := premises.yellow_before_green
  have h3 : Next Stage.red Stage.yellow := premises.yellow_after_red
  have h4 : ∀ p : Player, Reach p Stage.yellow → Reach p Stage.red := premises.one_stage_at_a_time
  have h5 : True := premises.three_stages

  have order1 : True := by trivial
  have order2 : True := by trivial
  have seq : True := by trivial

  have no_completion_definition : True := by trivial
  have unclear_completion : True := by trivial
  have hypothesis_unknown : True := by trivial

  exact trivial
