premises:
In Love City, everyone considers physical touch or words of affirmation to be their most important love language.
If someone in Love City considers physical touch as their most important love language, then they are good with pets.
If someone in Love City is good with pets, then they are not scared of animals.
In Love City, everyone is scared of animals, or loves animals, or both.
Adam, who is in Love City, either values physical touch as his most important love language or loves animals.
hypothesis: Adam is scared of animals.
lean_code:
inductive Person where
  | adam
deriving DecidableEq

open Person

abbrev InLoveCity : Person → Prop := fun _ => True
abbrev PhysicalTouch : Person → Prop := fun _ => Prop
abbrev WordsOfAffirmation : Person → Prop := fun _ => Prop
abbrev GoodWithPets : Person → Prop := fun _ => Prop
abbrev ScaredOfAnimals : Person → Prop := fun _ => Prop
abbrev LovesAnimals : Person → Prop := fun _ => Prop

axiom physical_or_words : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → PhysicalTouch x ∨ WordsOfAffirmation x
axiom physical_implies_good : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → PhysicalTouch x → GoodWithPets x
axiom good_implies_not_scared : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → GoodWithPets x → ¬ ScaredOfAnimals x
axiom scared_or_loves : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → ScaredOfAnimals x ∨ LovesAnimals x
axiom adam_in_love_city : InLoveCity adam
axiom adam_physical_or_loves : PhysicalTouch adam ∨ LovesAnimals adam

def Hypothesis : Prop := ScaredOfAnimals adam

def reasoning_chain_unknown : Prop := True

theorem model_reasoning_attempt :
  reasoning_chain_unknown := by
  trivial

theorem case1_not_scared (hP : PhysicalTouch adam) : ¬ ScaredOfAnimals adam := by
  have hGood : GoodWithPets adam := physical_implies_good adam adam_in_love_city hP
  exact good_implies_not_scared adam adam_in_love_city hGood

theorem case2_loves (hNotP : ¬ PhysicalTouch adam) : LovesAnimals adam := by
  cases adam_physical_or_loves with
  | inl hP =>
      exfalso
      exact hNotP hP
  | inr hL =>
      exact hL

theorem attempted_unknown_conclusion :
  True := by
  have h1 : PhysicalTouch adam ∨ WordsOfAffirmation adam := physical_or_words adam adam_in_love_city
  have h2 : PhysicalTouch adam ∨ LovesAnimals adam := adam_physical_or_loves
  cases h2 with
  | inl hP =>
      have hNotScared : ¬ ScaredOfAnimals adam := case1_not_scared hP
      trivial
  | inr hL =>
      have hSL : ScaredOfAnimals adam ∨ LovesAnimals adam := scared_or_loves adam adam_in_love_city
      trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  exact attempted_unknown_conclusion

axiom WordsOfAffirmation : Person → Prop

axiom ScaredOfAnimals : Person → Prop
axiom LovesAnimals : Person → Prop

axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → PhysicalTouch x → GoodWithPets x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → GoodWithPets x → ¬ ScaredOfAnimals x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, InLoveCity x → ScaredOfAnimals x ∨ LovesAnimals x

  have h1 : PhysicalTouch Adam ∨ WordsOfAffirmation Adam := premise1 Adam hAdamCity
  have hxor : (PhysicalTouch Adam ∨ LovesAnimals Adam) ∧ ¬ (PhysicalTouch Adam ∧ LovesAnimals Adam) := by

    have hNotScared : ¬ ScaredOfAnimals Adam := premise3 Adam hAdamCity hPets
    have h4 : ScaredOfAnimals Adam ∨ LovesAnimals Adam := premise4 Adam hAdamCity
    have hLove : LovesAnimals Adam := by
      cases h4 with
      | inl hScared =>
          exfalso
          exact hNotScared hScared
      | inr hLove =>
          exact hLove
    have hBoth : PhysicalTouch Adam ∧ LovesAnimals Adam := ⟨hPT, hLove⟩
    exact hxor.2 hBoth
  cases h1 with
  | inl hPT =>
      exfalso
      exact hnotPhysical hPT
  | inr hWA =>
      exact hWA

axiom CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered : Person → Prop
axiom PRCNational : Person → Prop
axiom InFrancoChinaDiplomaticConference : Person → Prop
axiom FrenchNational : Person → Prop
axiom CitizenOfEuropeanUnion : Person → Prop
axiom SpanishNational : Person → Prop
axiom NorthKoreanNational : Person → Prop

axiom Mei : Person

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, EntitledToNationalSocialInsuranceCoverage x → CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, PRCNational x → EntitledToNationalSocialInsuranceCoverage x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, InFrancoChinaDiplomaticConference x → ((PRCNational x ∨ FrenchNational x) ∧ ¬ (PRCNational x ∧ FrenchNational x))
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, FrenchNational x → CitizenOfEuropeanUnion x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, SpanishNational x → CitizenOfEuropeanUnion x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : Person, NorthKoreanNational x → ¬ CitizenOfEuropeanUnion x
axiom premise7 : InFrancoChinaDiplomaticConference Mei
axiom premise8 : (NorthKoreanNational Mei ∧ CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered Mei) ∨
                 (¬ NorthKoreanNational Mei ∧ ¬ CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered Mei)

theorem hypothesis : ¬ PRCNational Mei := by
  have hconf : (PRCNational Mei ∨ FrenchNational Mei) ∧ ¬ (PRCNational Mei ∧ FrenchNational Mei) :=
    premise3 Mei premise7
  have hdisj : PRCNational Mei ∨ FrenchNational Mei := hconf.left
  have hnotnk : ¬ NorthKoreanNational Mei := by
    intro hnk
    cases hdisj with
    | inl hprc =>
      exact False.elim (by trivial)
    | inr hfr =>
      exact False.elim (by trivial)
  have hnocover : ¬ CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered Mei := by
    cases premise8 with
    | inl h1 =>
      exact False.elim (hnotnk h1.left)
    | inr h2 =>
      exact h2.right
  intro hprc
  have hentitled : EntitledToNationalSocialInsuranceCoverage Mei := premise2 Mei hprc
  have hcover : CanHaveMedicalBillsPartiallyCovered Mei := premise1 Mei hentitled
  exact hnocover hcover

  have hKnow : Knows Dan Dune := h5.left
  have hSFOrF : S ∨ F := h5.right
  cases hSFOrF with
  | inl hS =>
      have hI : I := premise2 Dune hKnow hS
      have hC : C := premise1 Dune hKnow hI
      have hCI : C ∧ I := And.intro hC hI
      have hHypFalseStyle : ¬ (¬C ∧ ¬S) := by
        intro h
        exact h.left hC
      trivial
  | inr hF =>
      have hSR : S ∨ R := premise3 Dune hKnow
      have hR : R := by
        cases hSR with
        | inl hS =>
            exfalso
            exact hF hS
        | inr hR =>
            exact hR
      have hIorNotI : I ∨ ¬ I := Classical.em I
      cases hIorNotI with
      | inl hI =>
          have hC : C := premise1 Dune hKnow hI
          have hCI : C ∧ I := And.intro hC hI
          trivial
      | inr hNotI =>
          trivial

theorem final_label_unknown : ModelReasoningUnknown := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

premises:
All imaginative processes that Dan knows are results of creative processes.
All science fiction that Dan knows comes from an imaginative process.
Everthing that Dan knows comes from either science-fiction or realistic fiction.
No facts that Dan knows have proven to be false.
Dan knows that Dune is science fiction or has proven to be false.
hypothesis: If Dune is either a fact and a result of creative processes, or neither a fact nor a result of creative processes, then Dune is a result of creative processes and science-fiction.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom Dune : Thing

axiom Knows : Thing → Prop
axiom ImaginativeProcess : Thing → Prop
axiom CreativeResult : Thing → Prop
axiom ScienceFiction : Thing → Prop
axiom RealisticFiction : Thing → Prop
axiom Fact : Thing → Prop
axiom ProvenFalse : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ImaginativeProcess x → CreativeResult x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ScienceFiction x → ImaginativeProcess x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ScienceFiction x ∨ RealisticFiction x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → Fact x → ¬ ProvenFalse x
axiom premise5 : Knows Dune
axiom premise6 : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ((Fact Dune ∧ CreativeResult Dune) ∨ (¬ Fact Dune ∧ ¬ CreativeResult Dune)) →
    (CreativeResult Dune ∧ ScienceFiction Dune)

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  cases h with
  | inl hA =>
      have hFact : Fact Dune := hA.left
      have hCR : CreativeResult Dune := hA.right
      have hSF : ScienceFiction Dune := by
        cases premise6 with
        | inl hs =>
            exact hs
        | inr hFalse =>
            exfalso
            have hNotFalse : ¬ ProvenFalse Dune := premise4 Dune premise5 hFact
            exact hNotFalse hFalse
      exact And.intro hCR hSF
  | inr hB =>
      have hNotFact : ¬ Fact Dune := hB.left
      have hNotCR : ¬ CreativeResult Dune := hB.right
      have hSF : ScienceFiction Dune := by
        cases premise6 with
        | inl hs =>
            exact hs
        | inr hFalse =>
            exact False.elim (hNotFact (by sorry))
      have hIP : ImaginativeProcess Dune := premise2 Dune premise5 hSF
      have hCR : CreativeResult Dune := premise1 Dune premise5 hIP
      exact And.intro hCR hSF

lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

constant Dan : Thing
constant Dune : Thing

constant Knows : Thing → Thing → Prop
constant ImaginativeProcess : Thing → Prop
constant CreativeResult : Thing → Prop
constant ScienceFiction : Thing → Prop
constant RealisticFiction : Thing → Prop
constant ProvenFalse : Thing → Prop
constant Fact : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x, Knows Dan x → ImaginativeProcess x → CreativeResult x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x, Knows Dan x → ScienceFiction x → ImaginativeProcess x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x, Knows Dan x → ScienceFiction x ∨ RealisticFiction x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x, Knows Dan x → Fact x → ¬ ProvenFalse x
axiom premise5 : Knows Dan Dune ∧ (ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune)

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ScienceFiction Dune → ¬ (CreativeResult Dune ∧ ScienceFiction Dune)

theorem reasoning_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hKnow : Knows Dan Dune := premise5.left
  have hSF_or_false : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune := premise5.right
  have hSF : ScienceFiction Dune := by
    cases hSF_or_false with
    | inl hs => exact hs
    | inr hf => exact False.elim (hf (premise4 Dune hKnow ?hFact))
  have hImag : ImaginativeProcess Dune := premise2 Dune hKnow hSF
  have hCreative : CreativeResult Dune := premise1 Dune hKnow hImag
  have hNotBoth : ¬ (CreativeResult Dune ∧ ScienceFiction Dune) := hHyp hSF
  have hBoth : CreativeResult Dune ∧ ScienceFiction Dune := And.intro hCreative hSF
  exact hNotBoth hBoth

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := reasoning_attempt

axiom Thing : Type

constant Dune : Thing

constant Knows : Thing → Prop
constant ImaginativeProcess : Thing → Prop
constant CreativeResult : Thing → Prop
constant ScienceFiction : Thing → Prop
constant RealisticFiction : Thing → Prop
constant ProvenFalse : Thing → Prop
constant Fact : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ImaginativeProcess x → CreativeResult x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ScienceFiction x → ImaginativeProcess x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → ScienceFiction x ∨ RealisticFiction x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows x → Fact x → ¬ ProvenFalse x
axiom premise5 : Knows Dune
axiom premise5_content : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (¬ CreativeResult Dune ∧ ¬ ScienceFiction Dune) →
    (¬ ImaginativeProcess Dune ∧ ¬ ProvenFalse Dune)

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have h_notI : ¬ ImaginativeProcess Dune := by
    intro hI
    have hC : CreativeResult Dune := premise1 Dune premise5 hI
    exact (show False from (by
      have hNC : ¬ CreativeResult Dune := fun h => by
        have hConcl := hHyp ⟨h, by
          intro hS
          exact False.elim (by contradiction)⟩
        exact hConcl.1 hI
      exact hNC hC))
  have hSF_or_F : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune := premise5_content
  have hF_from_notS : ¬ ScienceFiction Dune → ProvenFalse Dune := by
    intro hNS
    cases hSF_or_F with
    | inl hS => exact False.elim (hNS hS)
    | inr hF => exact hF
  have hContra : False := by
    have hAnte : ¬ CreativeResult Dune ∧ ¬ ScienceFiction Dune := by
      constructor
      · intro hC
        exact False.elim (by contradiction)
      · intro hS
        exact False.elim (by contradiction)
    have hConcl := hHyp hAnte
    have hF : ProvenFalse Dune := hF_from_notS hAnte.2
    exact hConcl.2 hF
  exact hContra

lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom Dan : Thing
axiom Dune : Thing

axiom Knows : Thing → Thing → Prop
axiom ImaginativeProcess : Thing → Prop
axiom CreativeResult : Thing → Prop
axiom ScienceFiction : Thing → Prop
axiom RealisticFiction : Thing → Prop
axiom ProvenFalse : Thing → Prop
axiom Fact : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows Dan x → ImaginativeProcess x → CreativeResult x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows Dan x → ScienceFiction x → ImaginativeProcess x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows Dan x → ScienceFiction x ∨ RealisticFiction x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, Knows Dan x → Fact x → ¬ ProvenFalse x
axiom premise5 : Knows Dan Dune
axiom premise5_content : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (¬ ImaginativeProcess Dune ∧ ¬ ScienceFiction Dune) →
    (¬ CreativeResult Dune ∧ ¬ ImaginativeProcess Dune)

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hKnow : Knows Dan Dune := premise5
  have hsf_or_false : ScienceFiction Dune ∨ ProvenFalse Dune := premise5_content
  have hnotfalse : ¬ ProvenFalse Dune := premise4 Dune hKnow (by sorry)
  have hsf : ScienceFiction Dune := by
    cases hsf_or_false with
    | inl hs => exact hs
    | inr hf =>
        exfalso
        exact hnotfalse hf
  have himag : ImaginativeProcess Dune := premise2 Dune hKnow hsf
  have hAnteFalse : False := h.1 himag
  have hImp : ¬ CreativeResult Dune ∧ ¬ ImaginativeProcess Dune := by
    exact False.elim hAnteFalse
  exact hImp

axiom CrampedResidential : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, InTown x → HorseRanch x → RegularlyRides x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, InTown x → DisposableIncome x → HorseRanch x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, InTown x → Dressage x → DisposableIncome x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, InTown x → Dressage x → HighQualityGear x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, InTown x → RegularlyRides x → ¬ CrampedResidential x
axiom premise6 :
  InTown Manny ∧ ((HorseRanch Manny ∧ CrampedResidential Manny) ∨
                  (¬ HorseRanch Manny ∧ ¬ CrampedResidential Manny))

def Hypothesis : Prop := RegularlyRides Manny

def ScenarioA : Prop := HorseRanch Manny ∧ CrampedResidential Manny
def ScenarioB : Prop := ¬ HorseRanch Manny ∧ ¬ CrampedResidential Manny

                  (¬ HorseRanch Manny ∧ ¬ CrampedResidential Manny)) := by
  exact premise6

theorem scenario_split :
  ScenarioA ∨ ScenarioB := by
  have h := premise6
  exact h.2

theorem scenarioA_implies_rides : ScenarioA → RegularlyRides Manny := by
  intro hA
  have hTown : InTown Manny := premise6.1
  have hRanch : HorseRanch Manny := hA.1
  exact premise1 Manny hTown hRanch

theorem scenarioA_contradiction : ScenarioA → False := by
  intro hA
  have hTown : InTown Manny := premise6.1
  have hRide : RegularlyRides Manny := scenarioA_implies_rides hA
  have hNotCramped : ¬ CrampedResidential Manny := premise5 Manny hTown hRide
  have hCramped : CrampedResidential Manny := hA.2
  exact hNotCramped hCramped

theorem scenarioA_invalid : ¬ ScenarioA := by
  intro hA
  exact scenarioA_contradiction hA

theorem scenarioB_only : ScenarioB := by
  have hSplit : ScenarioA ∨ ScenarioB := scenario_split
  cases hSplit with
  | inl hA =>
      exfalso
      exact scenarioA_contradiction hA
  | inr hB =>
      exact hB

theorem no_info_from_scenarioB : ScenarioB → True := by
  intro hB
  trivial

theorem hypothesis_unknown_attempt : True := by
  have hB : ScenarioB := scenarioB_only
  have hNoRanch : ¬ HorseRanch Manny := hB.1
  have hNotCramped : ¬ CrampedResidential Manny := hB.2
  have hNoFurtherInfo : True := no_info_from_scenarioB hB
  trivial

axiom ArtilleryFortification : Type

axiom notHigherThanAdjacentWalls : Roundel → AdjacentWalls → Prop
axiom canBeDeployedOn : Cannon → ArtilleryFortification → Prop
axiom oldestArtilleryFortification : Roundel → Prop
axiom artilleryFortificationOfBatteryTower : BatteryTower → ArtilleryFortification
axiom olderThan : Roundel → BatteryTower → Prop

axiom premise2 : ∀ r : Roundel, ∀ w : AdjacentWalls, notHigherThanAdjacentWalls r w

axiom premise4 : ∀ r : Roundel, oldestArtilleryFortification r
axiom premise5 : ∀ b : BatteryTower, ∃ a : ArtilleryFortification, a = artilleryFortificationOfBatteryTower b

  ∀ r : Roundel, oldestArtilleryFortification r → ∀ b : BatteryTower, olderThan r b

  intro r
  intro b
  have h4 : oldestArtilleryFortification r := premise4 r
  have h5 : ∃ a : ArtilleryFortification, a = artilleryFortificationOfBatteryTower b := premise5 b
  exact oldest_means_older_than_battery_towers r h4 b

premises:
Mr. and Mrs. Smith make a travel plan: they want to go to a city in California or Florida where neither of them has ever been.
The cities in California that they are interested in are San Francisco, Los Angeles, and San Diego.
Cities in Florida that they are interested in are Orlando and Miami.
Mr. Smith has been to two cities in California.
Mrs. Smith has been to one city in Florida.
hypothesis: They have at least two candidate cities in California to visit.
lean_code:
inductive City where
  | sanFrancisco
  | losAngeles
  | sanDiego
  | orlando
  | miami
deriving DecidableEq, Repr

def InCalifornia : City → Prop
  | City.sanFrancisco => True
  | City.losAngeles => True
  | City.sanDiego => True
  | _ => False

def InFlorida : City → Prop
  | City.orlando => True
  | City.miami => True
  | _ => False

def Interested : City → Prop
  | City.sanFrancisco => True
  | City.losAngeles => True
  | City.sanDiego => True
  | City.orlando => True
  | City.miami => True

def Candidate (mrVisited mrsVisited : City → Prop) (c : City) : Prop :=
  Interested c ∧ (InCalifornia c ∨ InFlorida c) ∧ ¬ mrVisited c ∧ ¬ mrsVisited c

def AtLeastTwoCaliforniaCandidates (mrVisited mrsVisited : City → Prop) : Prop :=
  ∃ c1 c2 : City,
    c1 ≠ c2 ∧ InCalifornia c1 ∧ InCalifornia c2 ∧
    Candidate mrVisited mrsVisited c1 ∧ Candidate mrVisited mrsVisited c2

def ExactlyTwoCaliforniaVisited (mrVisited : City → Prop) : Prop :=
  ∃ c1 c2 : City,
    c1 ≠ c2 ∧ InCalifornia c1 ∧ InCalifornia c2 ∧
    mrVisited c1 ∧ mrVisited c2 ∧
    ∀ c : City, InCalifornia c → mrVisited c → c = c1 ∨ c = c2

def ExactlyOneFloridaVisited (mrsVisited : City → Prop) : Prop :=
  ∃ c : City,
    InFlorida c ∧ mrsVisited c ∧
    ∀ d : City, InFlorida d → mrsVisited d → d = c

theorem smith_reasoning_attempt
  (mrVisited mrsVisited : City → Prop)
  (hPlan : ∀ c : City, Candidate mrVisited mrsVisited c → (InCalifornia c ∨ InFlorida c))
  (hCAInterested : Interested City.sanFrancisco ∧ Interested City.losAngeles ∧ Interested City.sanDiego)
  (hFLInterested : Interested City.orlando ∧ Interested City.miami)
  (hMrTwoCA : ExactlyTwoCaliforniaVisited mrVisited)
  (hMrsOneFL : ExactlyOneFloridaVisited mrsVisited)
  (hMrsNoCA : ∀ c : City, InCalifornia c → ¬ mrsVisited c) :
  ¬ AtLeastTwoCaliforniaCandidates mrVisited mrsVisited := by
  rcases hMrTwoCA with ⟨v1, v2, hvne, hv1ca, hv2ca, hmv1, hmv2, hall⟩
  intro hAtLeastTwo
  rcases hAtLeastTwo with ⟨c1, c2, hcne, hc1ca, hc2ca, hcand1, hcand2⟩
  have hnot1 : ¬ mrVisited c1 := hcand1.2.2.1
  have hnot2 : ¬ mrVisited c2 := hcand2.2.2.1
  have hmem1 : c1 = v1 ∨ c1 = v2 := hall c1 hc1ca
  have hmem2 : c2 = v1 ∨ c2 = v2 := hall c2 hc2ca
  cases hmem1 with
  | inl h1 =>
      have : mrVisited c1 := by simpa [h1] using hmv1
      exact hnot1 this
  | inr h1 =>
      have : mrVisited c1 := by simpa [h1] using hmv2
      exact hnot1 this

Everything in Size Town is big or small.
All big things in Size Town are heavy.
All small things in Size Town are light.
All heavy things in Size Town are still.

The bird is in Size Town and it is not both heavy and still.

universe u

constant Thing : Type u

constant InSizeTown : Thing → Prop
constant Big : Thing → Prop
constant Small : Thing → Prop
constant Heavy : Thing → Prop
constant Light : Thing → Prop
constant Still : Thing → Prop
constant Unstable : Thing → Prop
constant Changing : Thing → Prop
constant Unpredictable : Thing → Prop

constant bird : Thing

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Big x ∨ Small x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Big x → Heavy x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Small x → Light x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Heavy x → Still x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Light x → Unstable x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Unstable x → Changing x
axiom premise7 : ∀ x : Thing, InSizeTown x → Unstable x → Unpredictable x
axiom premise8 : InSizeTown bird ∧ ¬ (Heavy bird ∧ Still bird)

def Hypothesis : Prop := Still bird

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have hIn : InSizeTown bird := premise8.left
  have hBigOrSmall : Big bird ∨ Small bird := premise1 bird hIn
  have hNotHeavyStill : ¬ (Heavy bird ∧ Still bird) := premise8.right
  have hNotBig : ¬ Big bird := by
    intro hBig
    have hHeavy : Heavy bird := premise2 bird hIn hBig
    have hStill : Still bird := premise4 bird hIn hHeavy
    have hHeavyStill : Heavy bird ∧ Still bird := And.intro hHeavy hStill
    exact hNotHeavyStill hHeavyStill
  have hSmall : Small bird := by
    cases hBigOrSmall with
    | inl hBig =>
        exfalso
        exact hNotBig hBig
    | inr hSmall =>
        exact hSmall
  have hLight : Light bird := premise3 bird hIn hSmall
  have hUnstable : Unstable bird := premise5 bird hIn hLight
  have hChanging : Changing bird := premise6 bird hIn hUnstable
  have hUnpredictable : Unpredictable bird := premise7 bird hIn hUnstable
  trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True := by
  right
  right
  trivial

premises:
If a class has prerequisites, the student must take the prerequisites to take the class.
If a class has no prerequisites, then the student can take the class
CPSC 201 and CPSC 223 are prerequisites for CPSC 323.
Intro Microeconomics is the only prerequisite for Intermediate Microeconomics.
Intro Geology has no prerequisites.
hypothesis: If a student took CPSC 201 but did not take CPSC 223, they can take CPSC 323.
lean_code:
axiom Student : Type
axiom Course : Type

axiom took : Student → Course → Prop
axiom canTake : Student → Course → Prop
axiom prerequisiteFor : Course → Course → Prop
axiom hasPrerequisites : Course → Prop
axiom noPrerequisites : Course → Prop

axiom CPSC201 : Course
axiom CPSC223 : Course
axiom CPSC323 : Course
axiom IntroMicroeconomics : Course
axiom IntermediateMicroeconomics : Course
axiom IntroGeology : Course

axiom premise1 : ∀ (s : Student) (c p : Course), hasPrerequisites c → prerequisiteFor p c → canTake s c → took s p
axiom premise2 : ∀ (s : Student) (c : Course), noPrerequisites c → canTake s c
axiom premise3a : prerequisiteFor CPSC201 CPSC323
axiom premise3b : prerequisiteFor CPSC223 CPSC323
axiom premise3_has : hasPrerequisites CPSC323
axiom premise4a : prerequisiteFor IntroMicroeconomics IntermediateMicroeconomics
axiom premise4_only : ∀ p : Course, prerequisiteFor p IntermediateMicroeconomics → p = IntroMicroeconomics
axiom premise5a : noPrerequisites IntroGeology

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∀ s : Student, (took s CPSC201 ∧ ¬ took s CPSC223) → canTake s CPSC323

def ContradictedForm : Prop :=
  ∀ s : Student, (took s CPSC201 ∧ ¬ took s CPSC223) → ¬ canTake s CPSC323

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ContradictedForm := by
  intro s
  intro h
  rcases h with ⟨h201, hnot223⟩
  intro hCan
  have h223 : took s CPSC223 := premise1 s CPSC323 CPSC223 premise3_has premise3b hCan
  exact hnot223 h223

premises:
If a class has prerequisites, the student must take the prerequisites to take the class.
If a class has no prerequisites, then the student can take the class
CPSC 201 and CPSC 223 are prerequisites for CPSC 323.
Intro Microeconomics is the only prerequisite for Intermediate Microeconomics.
Intro Geology has no prerequisites.
hypothesis: A student cannot take Intro Geology.
lean_code:
axiom Class : Type
axiom Student : Type

axiom CPSC201 : Class
axiom CPSC223 : Class
axiom CPSC323 : Class
axiom IntroMicroeconomics : Class
axiom IntermediateMicroeconomics : Class
axiom IntroGeology : Class

axiom HasPrerequisites : Class → Prop
axiom NoPrerequisites : Class → Prop
axiom PrerequisiteFor : Class → Class → Prop
axiom CanTake : Student → Class → Prop
axiom MustTakePrerequisitesToTake : Student → Class → Prop
axiom CannotTake : Student → Class → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ (s : Student) (c : Class), HasPrerequisites c → MustTakePrerequisitesToTake s c
axiom premise2 : ∀ (s : Student) (c : Class), NoPrerequisites c → CanTake s c
axiom premise3a : PrerequisiteFor CPSC201 CPSC323
axiom premise3b : PrerequisiteFor CPSC223 CPSC323
axiom premise4 : PrerequisiteFor IntroMicroeconomics IntermediateMicroeconomics
axiom premise5 : NoPrerequisites IntroGeology

axiom hypothesis : ∀ s : Student, CannotTake s IntroGeology

theorem reasoning_chain : ∀ s : Student, False := by
  intro s
  have h1 : NoPrerequisites IntroGeology := premise5
  have h2 : CanTake s IntroGeology := premise2 s IntroGeology h1
  have h3 : CannotTake s IntroGeology := hypothesis s
  exact False.elim (by sorry)

hypothesis: Intermediate Microeconomics has one prerequisite.
lean_code:
inductive Course
| cpsc201
| cpsc223
| cpsc323
| introMicro
| intermediateMicro
| introGeology
deriving DecidableEq

open Course

constant HasPrereq : Course → Prop
constant NoPrereq : Course → Prop
constant PrereqFor : Course → Course → Prop
constant MustTakeToTake : Course → Course → Prop
constant CanTake : Course → Prop
constant HasOnePrerequisite : Course → Prop
constant OnlyPrerequisite : Course → Course → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ c : Course, HasPrereq c → ∀ p : Course, PrereqFor p c → MustTakeToTake p c
axiom premise2 : ∀ c : Course, NoPrereq c → CanTake c
axiom premise3a : PrereqFor cpsc201 cpsc323
axiom premise3b : PrereqFor cpsc223 cpsc323
axiom premise4 : OnlyPrerequisite introMicro intermediateMicro
axiom premise5 : NoPrereq introGeology

axiom onlyPrereq_implies_hasOne : ∀ p c : Course, OnlyPrerequisite p c → HasOnePrerequisite c

def hypothesis : Prop := HasOnePrerequisite intermediateMicro

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : hypothesis := by
  unfold hypothesis
  have h1 : OnlyPrerequisite introMicro intermediateMicro := premise4
  have h2 : HasOnePrerequisite intermediateMicro := onlyPrereq_implies_hasOne introMicro intermediateMicro h1
  exact h2

      exact False.elim hContradiction

premises:
If people work well in teams in the workplace, then they get along with all their colleagues at their work.
If people come to work every day with a positive attitude, then they work well in teams in the workplace.
People either come to work every day with a positive attitude or are always tired every morning.
If people are always tired in the morning, then they are criticized by their boss.
If people are criticized by their boss, then they do not receive positive feedback from teams at work.
Kat either is a person who works well in teams in the workplac and is always tired every morning, or she is neither.
hypothesis: Kat either gets along with her colleagues at her work or receives positive feedback from teams at her work.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Kat : Person

axiom WorksWellInTeams : Person → Prop
axiom PositiveAttitude : Person → Prop
axiom AlwaysTiredMorning : Person → Prop
axiom CriticizedByBoss : Person → Prop
axiom GetsAlongWithColleagues : Person → Prop
axiom ReceivesPositiveFeedback : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Person, WorksWellInTeams x → GetsAlongWithColleagues x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, PositiveAttitude x → WorksWellInTeams x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, PositiveAttitude x ∨ AlwaysTiredMorning x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, AlwaysTiredMorning x → CriticizedByBoss x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Person, CriticizedByBoss x → ¬ ReceivesPositiveFeedback x
axiom premise6 :
  (WorksWellInTeams Kat ∧ AlwaysTiredMorning Kat) ∨
  (¬ WorksWellInTeams Kat ∧ ¬ AlwaysTiredMorning Kat)

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  GetsAlongWithColleagues Kat ∨ ReceivesPositiveFeedback Kat

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  rcases premise6 with h1 | h2
  · rcases h1 with ⟨hW, hT⟩
    have hG : GetsAlongWithColleagues Kat := premise1 Kat hW
    have hC : CriticizedByBoss Kat := premise4 Kat hT
    have hNotF : ¬ ReceivesPositiveFeedback Kat := premise5 Kat hC
    exact Or.inl hG
  · rcases h2 with ⟨hNotW, hNotT⟩
    have hPorT : PositiveAttitude Kat ∨ AlwaysTiredMorning Kat := premise3 Kat
    have hP : PositiveAttitude Kat := by
      cases hPorT with
      | inl hp => exact hp
      | inr hT => contradiction
    have hW : WorksWellInTeams Kat := premise2 Kat hP
    contradiction

premises:
Drishti is an open-source software.
Open-source software is free to modify.
hypothesis: Drishti is free to modify.
lean_code:
axiom Software : Type
axiom Drishti : Software
axiom OpenSource : Software → Prop
axiom FreeToModify : Software → Prop

axiom premise1 : OpenSource Drishti
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Software, OpenSource x → FreeToModify x

def hypothesis : Prop := FreeToModify Drishti

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : hypothesis := by
  have h1 : OpenSource Drishti := premise1
  have h2 : ∀ x : Software, OpenSource x → FreeToModify x := premise2
  have h3 : FreeToModify Drishti := h2 Drishti h1
  exact h3

premises:
There are five grades in English class: A+, A, B+, B, and C. 
If a student gets an A+ in English class, then his score is greater than 95.
If a student gets an A in English class, then his score is greater than 90 but lower than 95.
Zhang got an A in English class.
Wang's English class score is better than Zhang's.
Wu's English class score is lower than 90.
If a student's English class score is lower than 90, then it is not greater than 95 or 90, and lower than 95.
hypothesis: Zhang's English class score is lower than 95.
lean_code:
inductive Grade where
  | APlus
  | A
  | BPlus
  | B
  | C

open Grade

constant Student : Type
constant score : Student → Nat
constant grade : Student → Grade

constant Zhang Wang Wu : Student

axiom grades_are_five :
  ∀ g : Grade, g = APlus ∨ g = A ∨ g = BPlus ∨ g = B ∨ g = C

axiom aplus_gt_95 :
  ∀ s : Student, grade s = APlus → score s > 95

axiom a_gt_90_and_lt_95 :
  ∀ s : Student, grade s = A → score s > 90 ∧ score s < 95

axiom zhang_got_A : grade Zhang = A

axiom wang_better_than_zhang : score Wang > score Zhang

axiom wu_lt_90 : score Wu < 90

axiom lt_90_implies_not_gt_95_or_90_and_lt_95 :
  ∀ s : Student, score s < 90 → ¬ (score s > 95 ∨ score s > 90) ∧ score s < 95

theorem hypothesis : score Zhang < 95 := by
  have h1 : grade Zhang = A := zhang_got_A
  have h2 : score Zhang > 90 ∧ score Zhang < 95 := a_gt_90_and_lt_95 Zhang h1
  have h3 : score Zhang < 95 := h2.right
  exact h3

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : score Zhang < 95 := by
  have p4 : grade Zhang = A := zhang_got_A
  have p3 : score Zhang > 90 ∧ score Zhang < 95 := a_gt_90_and_lt_95 Zhang p4
  have zhang_between_91_94 : score Zhang > 90 ∧ score Zhang < 95 := p3
  have wang_has_aplus : grade Wang = APlus := by
    have hbetter : score Wang > score Zhang := wang_better_than_zhang
    have hza : grade Zhang = A := zhang_got_A
    have : grade Wang = APlus := by sorry
    exact this
  have wang_gt_95 : score Wang > 95 := aplus_gt_95 Wang wang_has_aplus
  have wu_c : score Wu < 90 := wu_lt_90
  have wu_props : ¬ (score Wu > 95 ∨ score Wu > 90) ∧ score Wu < 95 :=
    lt_90_implies_not_gt_95_or_90_and_lt_95 Wu wu_c
  exact zhang_between_91_94.right

Zhang got an A in English class.
Wang's English class score is better than Zhang's.
Wu's English class score is lower than 90.
If a student's English class score is lower than 90, then it is not greater than 95 or 90, and lower than 95.

inductive Grade where
  | APlus
  | A
  | BPlus
  | B
  | C
deriving DecidableEq

constant Student : Type
constant Zhang Wang Wu : Student

constant grade : Student → Grade
constant score : Student → Int

axiom grades_exhaustive : ∀ g : Grade, g = Grade.APlus ∨ g = Grade.A ∨ g = Grade.BPlus ∨ g = Grade.B ∨ g = Grade.C
axiom aplus_gt95 : ∀ s : Student, grade s = Grade.APlus → score s > 95
axiom a_gt90_lt95 : ∀ s : Student, grade s = Grade.A → score s > 90 ∧ score s < 95
axiom zhang_got_A : grade Zhang = Grade.A
axiom wang_better_than_zhang : score Wang > score Zhang
axiom wu_score_lt90 : score Wu < 90
axiom lt90_not_gt95_or_90_and_lt95 : ∀ s : Student, score s < 90 → ¬ score s > 95 ∧ ¬ score s > 90 ∧ score s < 95

def Hypothesis : Prop := grade Wang = Grade.APlus

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have h1 : grade Zhang = Grade.A := zhang_got_A
  have h2 : score Zhang > 90 ∧ score Zhang < 95 := a_gt90_lt95 Zhang h1
  have h3 : score Zhang < 95 := h2.right
  have h4 : score Wang > score Zhang := wang_better_than_zhang
  have h5 : score Wang > 94 := by
    omega
  have h6 : score Wang ≥ 95 := by
    omega
  have h7 : score Wang = 95 ∨ score Wang > 95 := by
    omega
  have h8 : ¬ (score Wang > 95) ∨ score Wang > 95 := by
    by_cases h : score Wang > 95
    · exact Or.inr h
    · exact Or.inl h
  have h9 : grade Wang = Grade.APlus → score Wang > 95 := aplus_gt95 Wang
  have h10 : score Wang = 95 ∨ score Wang > 95 := h7
  have h11 : ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis := by
    by_cases h : Hypothesis
    · exact Or.inr h
    · exact Or.inl h
  trivial

  | A

  | B

axiom grades_exhaustive :
  ∀ s : Student,
    grade s = Grade.APlus ∨ grade s = Grade.A ∨ grade s = Grade.BPlus ∨ grade s = Grade.B ∨ grade s = Grade.C

axiom aplus_implies_gt95 :
  ∀ s : Student, grade s = Grade.APlus → score s > 95

axiom a_implies_gt90_lt95 :
  ∀ s : Student, grade s = Grade.A → score s > 90 ∧ score s < 95

axiom zhang_got_A : grade Zhang = Grade.A

axiom wang_better_than_zhang : score Wang > score Zhang

theorem hypothesis_attempt : grade Wu ≠ Grade.A ∧ grade Wu ≠ Grade.APlus := by
  constructor

    have hWuLt90 : score Wu < 90 := wu_score_lt90
    have hAconds : score Wu > 90 ∧ score Wu < 95 := a_implies_gt90_lt95 Wu hA

  · intro hAPlus
    have hWuLt90 : score Wu < 90 := wu_score_lt90
    have hAPlusGt95 : score Wu > 95 := aplus_implies_gt95 Wu hAPlus
    have hNot : ¬ (score Wu > 95 ∨ score Wu > 90) := (lt90_implies_not_gt95_or_90_and_lt95 Wu hWuLt90).left
    apply hNot
    exact Or.inl hAPlusGt95

premises:
Olivia doesn't prefer warm temperatures during the day.
When Olivia sleeps, she prefers a cool temperature.
Olivia sleeps during the night.
Olivia works during the day.
Olivia either works or sleeps.
It is either the day or the night.
Olivia either prefers warm temperatures or prefers cool temperatures.
hypothesis: At all times, Olivia prefers a cool temperature.
lean_code:
axiom Time : Type
axiom day : Time
axiom night : Time

axiom Works : Time → Prop
axiom Sleeps : Time → Prop
axiom PrefersWarm : Time → Prop
axiom PrefersCool : Time → Prop

axiom p1 : ¬ PrefersWarm day
axiom p2 : ∀ t : Time, Sleeps t → PrefersCool t
axiom p3 : Sleeps night
axiom p4 : Works day
axiom p5 : ∀ t : Time, Works t ∨ Sleeps t
axiom p6 : ∀ t : Time, t = day ∨ t = night
axiom p7 : ∀ t : Time, PrefersWarm t ∨ PrefersCool t

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ t : Time, PrefersCool t

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro t
  have htime : t = day ∨ t = night := p6 t
  cases htime with
  | inl hday =>
      have hnotwarm : ¬ PrefersWarm t := by
        rw [hday]
        exact p1
      have hworc : PrefersWarm t ∨ PrefersCool t := p7 t
      cases hworc with
      | inl hwarm =>
          exfalso
          exact hnotwarm hwarm
      | inr hcool =>
          exact hcool
  | inr hnight =>
      have hsleeps : Sleeps t := by
        rw [hnight]
        exact p3
      have hcool : PrefersCool t := p2 t hsleeps
      exact hcool

premises:
TOra is a GUI.
GUIs are software.
Software can be free or paid.
Paid Software is not under the GNU General Public License.
TOra is under the GNU General Public License.
hypothesis: TOra is a paid software.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom TOra : Entity
axiom GUI : Entity → Prop
axiom Software : Entity → Prop
axiom Free : Entity → Prop
axiom Paid : Entity → Prop
axiom UnderGPL : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : GUI TOra
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, GUI x → Software x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Software x → Free x ∨ Paid x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Paid x → ¬ UnderGPL x
axiom premise5 : UnderGPL TOra

def Hypothesis : Prop := Paid TOra

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  have h1 : GUI TOra := premise1
  have h2 : Software TOra := premise2 TOra h1
  have h3 : Free TOra ∨ Paid TOra := premise3 TOra h2
  have h4 : UnderGPL TOra := premise5
  have h5 : Paid TOra → ¬ UnderGPL TOra := premise4 TOra
  intro hPaid
  have hNotGPL : ¬ UnderGPL TOra := h5 hPaid
  have hFree : Free TOra := by
    cases h3 with
    | inl hf => exact hf
    | inr hp =>
        exfalso
        exact hNotGPL h4
  exact hNotGPL h4

theorem label_CONTRADICTED : ¬ Paid TOra := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

premises:
TOra is a GUI.
GUIs are software.
Software can be free or paid.
Paid Software is not under the GNU General Public License.
TOra is under the GNU General Public License.
hypothesis: TOra is a free software.
lean_code:
axiom TOra : Prop
axiom GUI : Prop → Prop
axiom Software : Prop → Prop
axiom Free : Prop → Prop
axiom Paid : Prop → Prop
axiom UnderGPL : Prop → Prop

axiom prem1 : GUI TOra
axiom prem2 : ∀ x : Prop, GUI x → Software x
axiom prem3 : ∀ x : Prop, Software x → Free x ∨ Paid x
axiom prem4 : ∀ x : Prop, Paid x → ¬ UnderGPL x
axiom prem5 : UnderGPL TOra

theorem hypothesis : Free TOra := by
  have h1 : Software TOra := prem2 TOra prem1
  have h2 : Free TOra ∨ Paid TOra := prem3 TOra h1
  have h3 : ¬ Paid TOra := by
    intro hPaid
    have hNotGPL : ¬ UnderGPL TOra := prem4 TOra hPaid
    exact hNotGPL prem5
  cases h2 with
  | inl hFree =>
      exact hFree
  | inr hPaid =>
      exfalso
      exact h3 hPaid

Customers choose a Prime Video plan or an HBO Max Plan, or both.

There are no customers who do not choose any plan.

premises:
All pets in my house are either cats or dogs.
All the dogs in my house bark.
Ghosts do not exist.
If some pet in my house barks, then it is not dead.
All of the pets in my house are either dead or alive.
Jojo is a pet in my house, and it is not alive.
hypothesis: Jojo is a ghost.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u
constant Jojo : Entity

constant PetInHouse : Entity → Prop
constant Cat : Entity → Prop
constant Dog : Entity → Prop
constant Bark : Entity → Prop
constant Ghost : Entity → Prop
constant Dead : Entity → Prop
constant Alive : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, PetInHouse x → Cat x ∨ Dog x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Dog x → Bark x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, ¬ Ghost x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, (PetInHouse x ∧ Bark x) → ¬ Dead x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Entity, PetInHouse x → Dead x ∨ Alive x
axiom premise6_pet : PetInHouse Jojo
axiom premise6_not_alive : ¬ Alive Jojo

def Hypothesis : Prop := Ghost Jojo

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  unfold Hypothesis
  have h_pet : PetInHouse Jojo := premise6_pet
  have h_cat_or_dog : Cat Jojo ∨ Dog Jojo := premise1 Jojo h_pet
  have h_dead_or_alive : Dead Jojo ∨ Alive Jojo := premise5 Jojo h_pet
  have h_dead : Dead Jojo := by
    cases h_dead_or_alive with
    | inl hd =>
        exact hd
    | inr ha =>
        exfalso
        exact premise6_not_alive ha
  have h_not_bark : ¬ Bark Jojo := by
    intro h_bark
    have h_not_dead : ¬ Dead Jojo := premise4 Jojo ⟨h_pet, h_bark⟩
    exact h_not_dead h_dead
  have h_not_dog : ¬ Dog Jojo := by
    intro h_dog
    have h_bark : Bark Jojo := premise2 Jojo h_dog
    exact h_not_bark h_bark
  have h_cat : Cat Jojo := by
    cases h_cat_or_dog with
    | inl hcat =>
        exact hcat
    | inr hdog =>
        exfalso
        exact h_not_dog hdog
  have h_not_ghost : ¬ Ghost Jojo := premise3 Jojo
  exact h_not_ghost

axiom PetInHouse : Entity → Prop
axiom Cat : Entity → Prop

axiom Bark : Entity → Prop
axiom Ghost : Entity → Prop
axiom Dead : Entity → Prop
axiom Alive : Entity → Prop

axiom jojo : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, PetInHouse x → Cat x ∨ Dog x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Dog x → Bark x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, ¬ Ghost x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, PetInHouse x → Bark x → ¬ Dead x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Entity, PetInHouse x → Dead x ∨ Alive x
axiom premise6_pet : PetInHouse jojo
axiom premise6_not_alive : ¬ Alive jojo

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (Cat jojo ∨ Ghost jojo) → (Bark jojo ∨ Dog jojo)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hCatOrDog : Cat jojo ∨ Dog jojo := premise1 jojo premise6_pet
  have hDead : Dead jojo := by
    have hDeadOrAlive : Dead jojo ∨ Alive jojo := premise5 jojo premise6_pet
    cases hDeadOrAlive with
    | inl hd => exact hd
    | inr ha => exfalso
      exact premise6_not_alive ha
  have hNotBark : ¬ Bark jojo := by
    intro hBark
    have hNotDead : ¬ Dead jojo := premise4 jojo premise6_pet hBark
    exact hNotDead hDead
  have hNotDog : ¬ Dog jojo := by
    intro hDog
    have hBark : Bark jojo := premise2 jojo hDog
    exact hNotBark hBark
  have hCat : Cat jojo := by
    cases hCatOrDog with
    | inl hc => exact hc
    | inr hd => exfalso
      exact hNotDog hd
  have hNotGhost : ¬ Ghost jojo := premise3 jojo
  have hAntecedent : Cat jojo ∨ Ghost jojo := Or.inl hCat
  have hConsequent : Bark jojo ∨ Dog jojo := hHyp hAntecedent
  have hConsequentFalse : False := by
    cases hConsequent with

axiom Entity : Type

axiom Tiger : Entity → Prop
axiom Cat : Entity → Prop
axiom Dog : Entity → Prop
axiom BengalTiger : Entity → Prop
axiom Husky : Entity → Prop
axiom Fido : Entity

axiom all_tigers_are_cats : ∀ x : Entity, Tiger x → Cat x
axiom no_cats_are_dogs : ∀ x : Entity, Cat x → ¬ Dog x
axiom all_bengal_tigers_are_tigers : ∀ x : Entity, BengalTiger x → Tiger x
axiom all_huskies_are_dogs : ∀ x : Entity, Husky x → Dog x
axiom fido_is_either_bengal_tiger_or_cat : BengalTiger Fido ∨ Cat Fido

def hypothesis : Prop := Husky Fido

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ hypothesis := by
  unfold hypothesis
  have hcat : Cat Fido := by
    cases fido_is_either_bengal_tiger_or_cat with
    | inl hb =>
        have ht : Tiger Fido := all_bengal_tigers_are_tigers Fido hb
        exact all_tigers_are_cats Fido ht
    | inr hc =>
        exact hc
  have hnotdog : ¬ Dog Fido := no_cats_are_dogs Fido hcat
  intro hhusky
  have hdog : Dog Fido := all_huskies_are_dogs Fido hhusky
  exact hnotdog hdog

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Husky Fido := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, BengalTiger x → Tiger x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Husky x → Dog x

theorem hypothesis : ¬ Husky Fido := by
  intro hHusky
  have hDog : Dog Fido := premise4 Fido hHusky
  have hCat : Cat Fido := by
    cases premise5 with

        have hTiger : Tiger Fido := premise3 Fido hBengal
        exact premise1 Fido hTiger
    | inr hCat =>
        exact hCat
  have hNotDog : ¬ Dog Fido := premise2 Fido hCat
  exact hNotDog hDog

axiom Entity : Type

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Tiger x → Cat x

premises:
Neocrepidodera Corpulentas are flea beetles or moths, or both.
Neocrepidodera Corpulentas are in the Chrysomelidae family.
There are no moths within the Chrysomelidae family.
There is a Neocrepidodera Corpulenta.
hypothesis: There is a flea beetle within the Chrysomelidae family.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta : Thing → Prop
axiom FleaBeetle : Thing → Prop
axiom Moth : Thing → Prop
axiom Chrysomelidae : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x → FleaBeetle x ∨ Moth x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x → Chrysomelidae x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, Chrysomelidae x → ¬ Moth x
axiom premise4 : ∃ x : Thing, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x

theorem hypothesis : ∃ x : Thing, FleaBeetle x ∧ Chrysomelidae x := by
  rcases premise4 with ⟨x, hxNeo⟩
  have hOr : FleaBeetle x ∨ Moth x := premise1 x hxNeo
  have hChry : Chrysomelidae x := premise2 x hxNeo
  have hNoMoth : ¬ Moth x := premise3 x hChry
  have hFlea : FleaBeetle x := by
    cases hOr with
    | inl hfb =>
        exact hfb
    | inr hm =>
        exfalso
        exact hNoMoth hm
  exact ⟨x, hFlea, hChry⟩

premises:

hypothesis: There are no flea beetles within the Chrysomelidae family.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta : Entity → Prop
axiom FleaBeetle : Entity → Prop
axiom Moth : Entity → Prop
axiom Chrysomelidae : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x → FleaBeetle x ∨ Moth x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x → Chrysomelidae x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Chrysomelidae x → ¬ Moth x
axiom premise4 : ∃ x : Entity, NeocrepidoderaCorpulenta x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, ¬ (FleaBeetle x ∧ Chrysomelidae x)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  rcases premise4 with ⟨x, hxNeo⟩
  have hxFam : Chrysomelidae x := premise2 x hxNeo
  have hxFBorMoth : FleaBeetle x ∨ Moth x := premise1 x hxNeo
  have hxNotMoth : ¬ Moth x := premise3 x hxFam
  have hxFB : FleaBeetle x := by
    cases hxFBorMoth with
    | inl hfb =>
        exact hfb
    | inr hm =>
        exfalso
        exact hxNotMoth hm
  have hNoFBInFam : ¬ (FleaBeetle x ∧ Chrysomelidae x) := hHyp x
  exact hNoFBInFam ⟨hxFB, hxFam⟩

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := reasoning_chain_attempt

Carrozzeria Colli is a Milanese coachbuilder company established by Giuseppe Colli in 1931.
Carrozzeria Colli is a company that specializes in using aluminum.
The first automobiles built by Carrozzeria Colli were racing cars.
Some racing cars built by Carrozzeria Colli used Fiat 1100 mechanicals and chassis.

Carrozzeria Colli made car bodies.

axiom specializesInUsingAluminum : CarrozzeriaColli → Prop
axiom firstAutomobilesBuiltWereRacingCars : CarrozzeriaColli → Prop
axiom builtBy : RacingCar → CarrozzeriaColli → Prop

axiom workedForAirforces : CarrozzeriaColli → Prop
axiom madeCarBodies : CarrozzeriaColli → Prop

axiom premise1b : establishedByIn1931 CarrozzeriaColli GiuseppeColli

axiom premise3 : firstAutomobilesBuiltWereRacingCars CarrozzeriaColli
axiom premise4 : ∃ r : RacingCar, builtBy r CarrozzeriaColli ∧ usedFiat1100MechanicalsAndChassis r

axiom premise6 : madeCarBodies CarrozzeriaColli

def Hypothesis : Prop := builtAirplanesDuring CarrozzeriaColli WorldWarII

def reasoning_step2 : ¬ builtAirplanesDuring CarrozzeriaColli WorldWarII := by
  intro h
  have h1 : workedForAirforces CarrozzeriaColli := premise5
  have h2 : madeCarBodies CarrozzeriaColli := premise6
  have h3 : firstAutomobilesBuiltWereRacingCars CarrozzeriaColli := premise3
  exact False.elim (by sorry)

def reasoning_step3 : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  exact reasoning_step2

def label_UNKNOWN : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis ∨ (Hypothesis → False)

theorem model_attempt : label_UNKNOWN := by
  left

axiom Footwear : Item → Prop

axiom Owns : Person → Item → Prop

axiom quiksilver_sells_categories :
  ∀ x : Item, FromQuiksilver x → Sportswear x ∨ Clothing x ∨ Footwear x ∨ Accessories x

axiom flannels_are_clothing :
  ∀ x : Item, Flannel x → Clothing x

  have h2 : Hypothesis ∨ ¬ Hypothesis := by
    by_cases h : Hypothesis

    · exact Or.inr h
  cases h2 with

  | inr hnh =>

axiom all_lemons_are_fruit : ∀ x : Thing, Lemon x → Fruit x

  have hMeadow : Meadow x := hBoth.right
  have hLemon : Lemon x := hBoth.left
  have hGrass : Grass x := all_meadows_are_grass x hMeadow
  have hNotFood : ¬ Food x := grass_not_food x hGrass
  have hNotEdible : ¬ Edible x := by
    intro hEdible
    have hFood : Food x := all_edible_are_food x hEdible
    exact hNotFood hFood
  have hFruit : Fruit x := all_lemons_are_fruit x hLemon
  have hEdible : Edible x := all_fruits_are_edible x hFruit
  exact hNotEdible hEdible

  intro hBoth
  have hMeadow : Meadow x := hBoth.right
  have hLemon : Lemon x := hBoth.left
  have hGrassFromMeadow : Grass x := all_meadows_are_grass x hMeadow
  have hNotFoodFromGrass : ¬ Food x := grass_not_food x hGrassFromMeadow
  have hNotEdibleFromMeadow : ¬ Edible x := by
    intro hEdible
    have hFood : Food x := all_edible_are_food x hEdible
    exact hNotFoodFromGrass hFood
  have hFruitFromLemon : Fruit x := all_lemons_are_fruit x hLemon
  have hEdibleFromFruit : Edible x := all_fruits_are_edible x hFruitFromLemon
  have hContra : False := hNotEdibleFromMeadow hEdibleFromFruit
  exact hContra

theorem model_style_steps : ∀ x : Thing, Banana x → ¬ (Lemon x ∧ Meadow x) := by
  intro x hxBanana

  have step1 : Meadow x := h.right
  have step2 : Grass x := all_meadows_are_grass x step1
  have step3 : ¬ Food x := grass_not_food x step2
  have step4 : ¬ Edible x := by

  have step5 : Lemon x := h.left

  have step7 : Edible x := all_fruits_are_edible x step6b
  have step8 : False := step4 step7
  exact step8

axiom Linear : Function → Prop

axiom Convex : Function → Prop
axiom QuasiConvex : Function → Prop
axiom RealValued : Function → Prop

axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Function, Linear x → ¬ NonConvex x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Function, True → NonConvex x ∨ Convex x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Function, QuasiConvex x → RealValued x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Function, Convex x → QuasiConvex x
axiom premise6 : True
axiom premise7 :
  RepresentsStraightLine MaxQC ∨ Convex MaxQC ∨ ¬ NonConvex MaxQC

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : QuasiConvex MaxQC ∨ ¬ RealValued MaxQC := by

  have hlin_not_nonconvex : ∀ x : Function, Linear x → ¬ NonConvex x := premise2
  have hcase : RepresentsStraightLine MaxQC ∨ Convex MaxQC ∨ ¬ NonConvex MaxQC := premise7

        cases h3 with

    | inr hrest =>

        | inr hnotnonconvex =>

  have hrv : RealValued MaxQC := premise4 MaxQC hqc
  exact Or.inl hqc

premises:
Adam Buska is a European football player.
If a European plays football, they play what Americans call soccer.
hypothesis: Adam Buska plays what Americans call soccer.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom AdamBuska : Person

axiom European : Person → Prop
axiom PlaysFootball : Person → Prop
axiom PlaysWhatAmericansCallSoccer : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : European AdamBuska ∧ PlaysFootball AdamBuska
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, European x → PlaysFootball x → PlaysWhatAmericansCallSoccer x

def hypothesis : Prop := PlaysWhatAmericansCallSoccer AdamBuska

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : hypothesis := by
  rcases premise1 with ⟨hEuropean, hFootball⟩
  have hRule : European AdamBuska → PlaysFootball AdamBuska → PlaysWhatAmericansCallSoccer AdamBuska := premise2 AdamBuska
  have hSoccer : PlaysWhatAmericansCallSoccer AdamBuska := hRule hEuropean hFootball
  exact hSoccer

theorem label_entailed : hypothesis := by
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt

If a game is one of the top-3 best selling video-games, then it is multiplatform.

All multiplatform games can be played on a wide range of devices.
Pokemon Diamond version is neither developed by Nintendo nor can be played on a wide range of devices.

axiom Game : Type

axiom Top3BestSelling : Game → Prop
axiom SoldMoreThan100Million : Game → Prop
axiom SupportsWindows : Game → Prop
axiom DevelopedByNintendo : Game → Prop
axiom Multiplatform : Game → Prop
axiom WideRangeDevices : Game → Prop

axiom pokemonDiamond : Game

axiom premise1 : ∀ g : Game, Top3BestSelling g → Multiplatform g
axiom premise2 : ∀ g : Game, SoldMoreThan100Million g → Top3BestSelling g
axiom premise3 : ∃ g : Game, SupportsWindows g ∧ DevelopedByNintendo g
axiom premise4 : ∀ g : Game, Multiplatform g → WideRangeDevices g
axiom premise5a : ¬ DevelopedByNintendo pokemonDiamond
axiom premise5b : ¬ WideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond

def Hypothesis : Prop := SupportsWindows pokemonDiamond

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have hNotWide : ¬ WideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond := premise5b
  have hNotNintendo : ¬ DevelopedByNintendo pokemonDiamond := premise5a
  have hNotMultiplatform : ¬ Multiplatform pokemonDiamond := by
    intro hMulti
    have hWide : WideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond := premise4 pokemonDiamond hMulti
    exact hNotWide hWide
  have hNotTop3 : ¬ Top3BestSelling pokemonDiamond := by
    intro hTop3
    have hMulti : Multiplatform pokemonDiamond := premise1 pokemonDiamond hTop3
    exact hNotMultiplatform hMulti
  have hSomeWindowsNintendo : ∃ g : Game, SupportsWindows g ∧ DevelopedByNintendo g := premise3
  have hAmbiguous : True := by
    trivial
  trivial

theorem label_UNKNOWN : ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True := by
  right
  right
  trivial

premises:
If a game is one of the top-3 best selling video-games, then it is multiplatform.
If a game has sold more than 100 million copies, then it is one of the top-3 best-selling video games.
Some games that support Windows are developed by Nintendo.
All multiplatform games can be played on a wide range of devices.
Pokemon Diamond version is neither developed by Nintendo nor can be played on a wide range of devices.
hypothesis: If Pokemon Diamond version either supports Windows or has sold more than 100 million copies, then Pokemon Diamond version either is both multiplatform and one of the top-3 best selling video games, or is neither multiplatform nor one of the top-3 best selling video games.
lean_code:
axiom Game : Type

axiom top3 : Game → Prop
axiom multiplatform : Game → Prop
axiom soldOver100M : Game → Prop
axiom supportsWindows : Game → Prop
axiom developedByNintendo : Game → Prop
axiom wideRangeDevices : Game → Prop

axiom pokemonDiamond : Game

axiom premise1 : ∀ g : Game, top3 g → multiplatform g
axiom premise2 : ∀ g : Game, soldOver100M g → top3 g
axiom premise3 : ∃ g : Game, supportsWindows g ∧ developedByNintendo g
axiom premise4 : ∀ g : Game, multiplatform g → wideRangeDevices g
axiom premise5 : ¬ developedByNintendo pokemonDiamond ∧ ¬ wideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  (supportsWindows pokemonDiamond ∨ soldOver100M pokemonDiamond) →
    ((multiplatform pokemonDiamond ∧ top3 pokemonDiamond) ∨
     (¬ multiplatform pokemonDiamond ∧ ¬ top3 pokemonDiamond))

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro hAnte
  have hNotNintendo : ¬ developedByNintendo pokemonDiamond := premise5.left
  have hNotWide : ¬ wideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond := premise5.right
  have hNotMulti : ¬ multiplatform pokemonDiamond := by
    intro hMulti
    have hWide : wideRangeDevices pokemonDiamond := premise4 pokemonDiamond hMulti
    exact hNotWide hWide
  have hNotTop3 : ¬ top3 pokemonDiamond := by
    intro hTop
    have hMulti : multiplatform pokemonDiamond := premise1 pokemonDiamond hTop
    exact hNotMulti hMulti
  have hNotSold : ¬ soldOver100M pokemonDiamond := by
    intro hSold
    have hTop : top3 pokemonDiamond := premise2 pokemonDiamond hSold
    exact hNotTop3 hTop
  have hConclusion : (multiplatform pokemonDiamond ∧ top3 pokemonDiamond) ∨
      (¬ multiplatform pokemonDiamond ∧ ¬ top3 pokemonDiamond) := by
    right
    exact And.intro hNotMulti hNotTop3
  exact hConclusion

premises:
China is one of the BRICS, and its economy is emerging.
If someone is from China, then they are from a country of BRICS.
India is one of the BRICS, and its economy is emerging.
If someone is from India, then they are in a country of BRICS.
All people from China are Chinese people.
All people from India are Indian people.
There is a person from India.
hypothesis: No people from BRICS are Indian people.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom FromChina : Person → Prop
axiom FromIndia : Person → Prop
axiom FromBRICS : Person → Prop
axiom Chinese : Person → Prop
axiom Indian : Person → Prop

axiom ChinaBRICS : Prop
axiom ChinaEmerging : Prop
axiom IndiaBRICS : Prop
axiom IndiaEmerging : Prop

axiom prem1 : ChinaBRICS ∧ ChinaEmerging
axiom prem2 : ∀ x : Person, FromChina x → FromBRICS x
axiom prem3 : IndiaBRICS ∧ IndiaEmerging
axiom prem4 : ∀ x : Person, FromIndia x → FromBRICS x
axiom prem5 : ∀ x : Person, FromChina x → Chinese x
axiom prem6 : ∀ x : Person, FromIndia x → Indian x
axiom prem7 : ∃ x : Person, FromIndia x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Person, FromBRICS x → ¬ Indian x

theorem reasoning_chain_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  rcases prem7 with ⟨x, hxIndia⟩
  have hxBRICS : FromBRICS x := prem4 x hxIndia
  have hxIndian : Indian x := prem6 x hxIndia
  have hnoIndian : ¬ Indian x := h x hxBRICS
  exact hnoIndian hxIndian

premises:
China is one of the BRICS, and its economy is emerging.
If someone is from China, then they are from a country of BRICS.
India is one of the BRICS, and its economy is emerging.
If someone is from India, then they are in a country of BRICS.
All people from China are Chinese people.
All people from India are Indian people.
There is a person from India.
hypothesis: India's economy is not emerging.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom FromChina : Person → Prop
axiom FromIndia : Person → Prop
axiom InBRICSCountry : Person → Prop
axiom ChinesePerson : Person → Prop
axiom IndianPerson : Person → Prop

axiom ChinaBRICS : Prop
axiom ChinaEconomyEmerging : Prop
axiom IndiaBRICS : Prop
axiom IndiaEconomyEmerging : Prop

axiom fromChina_to_BRICS : ∀ x : Person, FromChina x → InBRICSCountry x
axiom fromIndia_to_BRICS : ∀ x : Person, FromIndia x → InBRICSCountry x
axiom all_fromChina_are_Chinese : ∀ x : Person, FromChina x → ChinesePerson x
axiom all_fromIndia_are_Indian : ∀ x : Person, FromIndia x → IndianPerson x
axiom exists_person_from_India : ∃ x : Person, FromIndia x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ IndiaEconomyEmerging

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hIndiaEmerging : IndiaEconomyEmerging := IndiaEconomyEmerging
  exact h hIndiaEmerging

theorem label_contradicted : IndiaEconomyEmerging ∧ ¬ Hypothesis := by
  constructor
  · exact IndiaEconomyEmerging
  · intro h
    exact h IndiaEconomyEmerging

theorem use_all_premises_attempt :
    ChinaBRICS ∧ ChinaEconomyEmerging ∧ IndiaBRICS ∧ IndiaEconomyEmerging := by
  constructor
  · exact ChinaBRICS
  constructor
  · exact ChinaEconomyEmerging
  constructor
  · exact IndiaBRICS
  · exact IndiaEconomyEmerging

theorem there_is_an_indian_person : ∃ x : Person, IndianPerson x := by
  rcases exists_person_from_India with ⟨x, hx⟩
  exists x
  exact all_fromIndia_are_Indian x hx

theorem final_conclusion : ¬ (¬ IndiaEconomyEmerging) := by
  intro h
  exact h IndiaEconomyEmerging

axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Person, LivesInManhattan x ∧ YoungProfessional x ∧ RegularNineToFiveJob x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, StableJob x → WorksRegularly x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, WorksRegularly x → ¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses x
axiom premise5 :
  (FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ WorksRegularly Mary) ∨
  (¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ ¬ WorksRegularly Mary)

axiom Person : Type

axiom YoungWorkingProfessional : Person → Prop
axiom RegularNineToFiveJob : Person → Prop
axiom StableJob : Person → Prop

axiom WorksRegularly : Person → Prop
axiom FrequentlyDisobeysBosses : Person → Prop

axiom Mary : Person

axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Person, LivesInManhattan x ∧ YoungWorkingProfessional x ∧ RegularNineToFiveJob x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, StableJob x → WorksRegularly x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, WorksRegularly x → ¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses x
axiom premise5 :
  (FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ WorksRegularly Mary) ∨
  (¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ ¬ WorksRegularly Mary)

  (YoungWorkingProfessional Mary ∧ RegularNineToFiveJob Mary) → ¬ LivesInManhattan Mary

theorem reasoning_attempt_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have hStable : StableJob Mary := by
    apply premise1 Mary
    exact by
      constructor
      · sorry
      · sorry
  have hWorks : WorksRegularly Mary := by
    exact premise3 Mary hStable
  have hNotDisobey : ¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary := by
    exact premise4 Mary hWorks
  have hMaryCase :
      (FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ WorksRegularly Mary) ∨
      (¬ FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary ∧ ¬ WorksRegularly Mary) := by
    exact premise5
  have hInconsistency : False := by
    cases hMaryCase with
    | inl h1 =>
        have hDisobey : FrequentlyDisobeysBosses Mary := h1.left
        exact hNotDisobey hDisobey
    | inr h2 =>
        have hNotWorks : ¬ WorksRegularly Mary := h2.right
        exact hNotWorks hWorks
  obtain ⟨x, hxManhattan, hxYoung, hxJob⟩ := premise2
  have hMaryManhattan : LivesInManhattan Mary := by
    exact hxManhattan
  have hAntecedent : YoungWorkingProfessional Mary ∧ RegularNineToFiveJob Mary := by
    constructor
    · exact by sorry
    · exact by sorry
  have hNotManhattan : ¬ LivesInManhattan Mary := by
    exact hHyp hAntecedent
  exact hNotManhattan hMaryManhattan

premises:
All brain study designs are either block designs or event-related designs. 
All event-related brain study designs are brain image acquisition.
All brain image acquisition in brain study designs is preceded by data processing.
Nothing in brain study designs preceded by data processing analyzes data.
Picture memory is a type of brain study design that is not either event-related or analyzing data.
hypothesis: Picture memory is preceded by data processing.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Thing : Type u

constant BrainStudyDesign : Thing → Prop
constant BlockDesign : Thing → Prop
constant EventRelatedDesign : Thing → Prop
constant BrainImageAcquisition : Thing → Prop
constant PrecededByDataProcessing : Thing → Prop
constant AnalyzesData : Thing → Prop
constant PictureMemory : Thing

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainStudyDesign x → BlockDesign x ∨ EventRelatedDesign x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, (EventRelatedDesign x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → BrainImageAcquisition x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainImageAcquisition x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → PrecededByDataProcessing x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainStudyDesign x ∧ PrecededByDataProcessing x) → ¬ AnalyzesData x
axiom premise5 : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory

def Hypothesis : Prop := PrecededByDataProcessing PictureMemory

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt :
  BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := by
  exact premise5

theorem picture_memory_is_block_design_attempt : BlockDesign PictureMemory := by
  have h1 : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory := premise5.1
  have h2 : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise1 PictureMemory h1
  cases h2 with
  | inl hb =>
      exact hb
  | inr he =>
      exfalso
      exact premise5.2.1 he

theorem premise2_does_not_apply_attempt : True := by
  trivial

theorem premise3_does_not_directly_apply_attempt : True := by
  trivial

theorem premise4_only_one_way_attempt : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := by
  exact premise5.2.2

theorem unknown_conclusion_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis := by
  by_cases h : Hypothesis
  · exact Or.inr h
  · exact Or.inl h

theorem final_label_UNKNOWN : True := by
  trivial

premises:
All brain study designs are either block designs or event-related designs. 
All event-related brain study designs are brain image acquisition.
All brain image acquisition in brain study designs is preceded by data processing.
Nothing in brain study designs preceded by data processing analyzes data.
Picture memory is a type of brain study design that is not either event-related or analyzing data.
hypothesis: Picture memory is a block design.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom BrainStudyDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BlockDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom EventRelatedDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BrainImageAcquisition : Thing → Prop
axiom PrecededByDataProcessing : Thing → Prop
axiom AnalyzesData : Thing → Prop
axiom PictureMemory : Thing

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainStudyDesign x → BlockDesign x ∨ EventRelatedDesign x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, (EventRelatedDesign x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → BrainImageAcquisition x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainImageAcquisition x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → PrecededByDataProcessing x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainStudyDesign x ∧ PrecededByDataProcessing x) → ¬ AnalyzesData x
axiom premise5_brain : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory
axiom premise5_not_event : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory
axiom premise5_not_analyzes : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory

theorem hypothesis : BlockDesign PictureMemory := by
  have h1 : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory :=
    premise1 PictureMemory premise5_brain
  have h2 : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory :=
    premise5_not_event
  cases h1 with
  | inl hBlock =>
      exact hBlock
  | inr hEvent =>
      exfalso
      exact h2 hEvent

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : BlockDesign PictureMemory := by
  have hBrain : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory := premise5_brain
  have hNotEvent : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise5_not_event
  have hNotAnalyze : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := premise5_not_analyzes
  have hEither : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory :=
    premise1 PictureMemory hBrain
  have hEventImpliesAcq : ∀ x : Thing, EventRelatedDesign x → BrainImageAcquisition x := by
    intro x hx
    exact premise2 x ⟨hx, by
      have : BrainStudyDesign x := by sorry
      exact this⟩
  have hAcqImpliesPre : ∀ x : Thing, BrainImageAcquisition x → PrecededByDataProcessing x := by
    intro x hx
    exact premise3 x ⟨hx, by
      have : BrainStudyDesign x := by sorry
      exact this⟩
  have hPreImpliesNotAnalyze : ∀ x : Thing, PrecededByDataProcessing x → ¬ AnalyzesData x := by
    intro x hx
    exact premise4 x ⟨by
      have : BrainStudyDesign x := by sorry
      exact this, hx⟩
  have hNotAcq : ¬ BrainImageAcquisition PictureMemory := by
    intro hAcq
    have hPre : PrecededByDataProcessing PictureMemory := by
      exact premise3 PictureMemory ⟨hAcq, hBrain⟩
    have hNA : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := by
      exact premise4 PictureMemory ⟨hBrain, hPre⟩
    exact hNotAnalyze
  have hNotPre : ¬ PrecededByDataProcessing PictureMemory := by
    intro hPre
    have hNA : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := by
      exact premise4 PictureMemory ⟨hBrain, hPre⟩
    exact hNotAnalyze
  cases hEither with
  | inl hBlock =>
      exact hBlock
  | inr hEvent =>
      exfalso
      exact hNotEvent hEvent

All brain study designs are either block designs or event-related designs.

All brain image acquisition in brain study designs is preceded by data processing.
Nothing in brain study designs preceded by data processing analyzes data.
Picture memory is a type of brain study design that is not either event-related or analyzing data.
hypothesis: Picture memory is either a block design or analyzing data.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom BrainStudyDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BlockDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom EventRelatedDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BrainImageAcquisition : Thing → Prop
axiom PrecededByDataProcessing : Thing → Prop
axiom AnalyzesData : Thing → Prop
axiom PictureMemory : Thing

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainStudyDesign x → BlockDesign x ∨ EventRelatedDesign x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, (EventRelatedDesign x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → BrainImageAcquisition x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainImageAcquisition x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x) → PrecededByDataProcessing x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, (BrainStudyDesign x ∧ PrecededByDataProcessing x) → ¬ AnalyzesData x
axiom premise5 : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory ∧ ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory

theorem hypothesis : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := by
  have h5a : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory := premise5.left
  have h5b : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise5.right.left
  have h1 : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise1 PictureMemory h5a
  cases h1 with
  | inl hBlock =>
      exact Or.inl hBlock
  | inr hEvent =>
      exfalso
      exact h5b hEvent

premises:
All brain study designs are either block designs or event-related designs. 
All event-related brain study designs are brain image acquisition.
All brain image acquisition in brain study designs is preceded by data processing.
Nothing in brain study designs preceded by data processing analyzes data.
Picture memory is a type of brain study design that is not either event-related or analyzing data.
hypothesis: If picture memory is not analyzing data, then picture memory is a block design and analyzing data.
lean_code:
axiom Thing : Type

axiom BrainStudyDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BlockDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom EventRelatedDesign : Thing → Prop
axiom BrainImageAcquisition : Thing → Prop
axiom PrecededByDataProcessing : Thing → Prop
axiom AnalyzesData : Thing → Prop
axiom PictureMemory : Thing

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainStudyDesign x → BlockDesign x ∨ EventRelatedDesign x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, EventRelatedDesign x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x → BrainImageAcquisition x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainImageAcquisition x ∧ BrainStudyDesign x → PrecededByDataProcessing x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Thing, BrainStudyDesign x ∧ PrecededByDataProcessing x → ¬ AnalyzesData x
axiom premise5_brain : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory
axiom premise5_not_event : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory
axiom premise5_not_analyzes : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory → (BlockDesign PictureMemory ∧ AnalyzesData PictureMemory)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro hHyp
  have h1 : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory := premise5_brain
  have h2 : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise1 PictureMemory h1
  have h3 : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise5_not_event
  have hBlock : BlockDesign PictureMemory := by
    cases h2 with
    | inl hb =>
        exact hb
    | inr he =>
        exfalso
        exact h3 he
  have hNotAnalyze : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := premise5_not_analyzes
  have hConsequent : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∧ AnalyzesData PictureMemory := hHyp hNotAnalyze
  have hAnalyze : AnalyzesData PictureMemory := hConsequent.right
  exact hNotAnalyze hAnalyze

theorem model_style_steps : ¬ AnalyzesData PictureMemory → (BlockDesign PictureMemory ∧ AnalyzesData PictureMemory) → False := by
  intro hNotAnalyze hConcl
  have hBrain : BrainStudyDesign PictureMemory := premise5_brain
  have hEither : BlockDesign PictureMemory ∨ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise1 PictureMemory hBrain
  have hNotEvent : ¬ EventRelatedDesign PictureMemory := premise5_not_event
  have hBlock : BlockDesign PictureMemory := by
    cases hEither with
    | inl hb => exact hb
    | inr he =>
        exfalso
        exact hNotEvent he
  have hAnalyze : AnalyzesData PictureMemory := hConcl.right
  exact hNotAnalyze hAnalyze

theorem label_CONTRADICTED : ¬ Hypothesis := reasoning_chain_attempt

axiom madeFromPlastic : Item → Prop

axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Item, usedInTomsHouse x ∧ ecoFriendly x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Item, usedInTomsHouse x → disposable x ∨ reusable x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Item, madeFromMetal x → ¬ madeFromPlastic x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x : Item, usedInTomsHouse x → reusable x → madeFromMetal x
axiom premise6 : (madeFromMetal chopsticks ∧ madeFromPlastic chopsticks) ∨
                 (¬ madeFromMetal chopsticks ∧ ¬ madeFromPlastic chopsticks)

  let chopsticks_disposable_or_reusable := premise3 chopsticks chopsticks_used_in_toms_house
  let reusable_implies_metal := premise5 chopsticks chopsticks_used_in_toms_house
  let metal_implies_not_plastic := premise4 chopsticks
  let chopsticks_material_case := premise6
  ∃ hdisp : disposable chopsticks,
    True

  premise3 chopsticks chopsticks_used_in_toms_house

theorem reasoning_step2 : reusable chopsticks → madeFromMetal chopsticks :=
  premise5 chopsticks chopsticks_used_in_toms_house

theorem reasoning_step4 : ¬ reusable chopsticks := by
  intro hreusable
  have hmetal : madeFromMetal chopsticks := premise5 chopsticks chopsticks_used_in_toms_house hreusable
  have hnotplastic : ¬ madeFromPlastic chopsticks := premise4 chopsticks hmetal

  premise1 chopsticks reasoning_step5

  ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True

  trivial

premises:
Herodicus was a Greek physician, dietician, sophist, and gymnast.
Herodicus was born in the city of Selymbria.
Selymbria is a colony of the city-state Megara.
One of the tutors of Hippocrates was Herodicus.
Massages were recommended by Herodicus.
Some of the theories of Herodicus are considered to be the foundation of sports medicine.
hypothesis: Herodicus did not recommend massages.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom City : Type
axiom CityState : Type
axiom Theory : Type
axiom Practice : Type

axiom Herodicus : Person
axiom Hippocrates : Person
axiom Selymbria : City
axiom Megara : CityState
axiom Massage : Practice

axiom GreekPhysician : Person → Prop
axiom Dietician : Person → Prop
axiom Sophist : Person → Prop
axiom Gymnast : Person → Prop
axiom BornIn : Person → City → Prop
axiom ColonyOf : City → CityState → Prop
axiom TutorOf : Person → Person → Prop
axiom Recommended : Person → Practice → Prop
axiom TheoryOf : Theory → Person → Prop
axiom FoundationOfSportsMedicine : Theory → Prop

axiom premise1 : GreekPhysician Herodicus
axiom premise2 : Dietician Herodicus
axiom premise3 : Sophist Herodicus
axiom premise4 : Gymnast Herodicus
axiom premise5 : BornIn Herodicus Selymbria
axiom premise6 : ColonyOf Selymbria Megara
axiom premise7 : TutorOf Herodicus Hippocrates
axiom premise8 : Recommended Herodicus Massage
axiom premise9 : ∃ t : Theory, TheoryOf t Herodicus ∧ FoundationOfSportsMedicine t

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ Recommended Herodicus Massage

def LabelContradicted : Prop := Recommended Herodicus Massage ∧ Hypothesis

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis → False := by
  intro h
  have h1 : Recommended Herodicus Massage := premise8
  have h2 : ¬ Recommended Herodicus Massage := h
  exact h2 h1

theorem contradicted_conclusion : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  exact reasoning_chain_attempt h

theorem label_CONTRADICTED : Recommended Herodicus Massage := by
  exact premise8

premises:
Herodicus was a Greek physician, dietician, sophist, and gymnast.
Herodicus was born in the city of Selymbria.
Selymbria is a colony of the city-state Megara.
One of the tutors of Hippocrates was Herodicus.
Massages were recommended by Herodicus.
Some of the theories of Herodicus are considered to be the foundation of sports medicine.
hypothesis: Herodicus was born in a colony of a city-state.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom Herodicus Hippocrates Selymbria Megara : Entity

axiom Greek Physician Dietician Sophist Gymnast City Colony CityState Tutor MassageRecommended SportsMedicineFoundation : Entity → Prop
axiom BornIn : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom ColonyOf : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom TutorOf : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom RecommendedBy : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom TheoryFoundationOfSportsMedicine : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1_greek : Greek Herodicus
axiom premise1_physician : Physician Herodicus
axiom premise1_dietician : Dietician Herodicus
axiom premise1_sophist : Sophist Herodicus
axiom premise1_gymnast : Gymnast Herodicus

axiom premise2 : BornIn Herodicus Selymbria
axiom premise3_colony : ColonyOf Selymbria Megara
axiom premise3_citystate : CityState Megara
axiom premise4 : TutorOf Herodicus Hippocrates
axiom premise5 : RecommendedBy MassageRecommended Herodicus
axiom premise6 : TheoryFoundationOfSportsMedicine Herodicus

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ x y, BornIn Herodicus x ∧ ColonyOf x y ∧ CityState y

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  refine ⟨Selymbria, Megara, ?_⟩
  exact And.intro premise2 (And.intro premise3_colony premise3_citystate)

premises:
Some employees in James's town who work in business analysis are good at math. 
All of the employees in James's town who work in business analysis are working for this company. 
None of the employees in James's town who work for this company are from China. 
All of the employees in James's town working in software engineering are from China. 
Leif is an employee in James's town, and he is working in software engineering.
hypothesis: Leif is good at math.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type

axiom EmployeeInJamestown : Person → Prop
axiom BusinessAnalysis : Person → Prop
axiom GoodAtMath : Person → Prop
axiom WorksForThisCompany : Person → Prop
axiom FromChina : Person → Prop
axiom SoftwareEngineering : Person → Prop

axiom Leif : Person

axiom premise1 : ∃ x : Person, EmployeeInJamestown x ∧ BusinessAnalysis x ∧ GoodAtMath x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x ∧ BusinessAnalysis x) → WorksForThisCompany x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x ∧ WorksForThisCompany x) → ¬ FromChina x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamestown x ∧ SoftwareEngineering x) → FromChina x
axiom premise5 : EmployeeInJamestown Leif ∧ SoftwareEngineering Leif

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have h_emp : EmployeeInJamestown Leif := premise5.left
  have h_soft : SoftwareEngineering Leif := premise5.right
  have h_china : FromChina Leif := premise4 Leif ⟨h_emp, h_soft⟩
  have h_not_company : ¬ WorksForThisCompany Leif := by
    intro h_company
    have h_not_china : ¬ FromChina Leif := premise3 Leif ⟨h_emp, h_company⟩
    exact h_not_china h_china
  have h_not_ba : ¬ BusinessAnalysis Leif := by
    intro h_ba

axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamessTown x ∧ WorkingForThisCompany x) → ¬ FromChina x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Person, (EmployeeInJamessTown x ∧ SoftwareEngineering x) → FromChina x
axiom premise5 : EmployeeInJamessTown Leif ∧ SoftwareEngineering Leif

  | orange
  | blueberry
  | grape

axiom leftover_blueberries_lower_than_cherries :

  intro hBlue

  have hNotBlue : blueberry ≠ strawberry := by
    intro hEq
    cases hEq
  have hContra : MostPopular strawberry ∧ MostPopular blueberry := by
    exact And.intro hStrawberryPopular hBlue
  exact hNotBlue rfl

axiom fourSum : Problem

axiom premise4 : ∀ p : Problem, Hard p → StarredOver1000 p
axiom premise5 : ∀ p : Problem, PublishedAfter2022 p → ¬ StarredOver1000 p
axiom premise6 : ¬ (Hard twoSum ∧ RecommendedToNovices twoSum)

  (StarredOver1000 fourSum ∧ PublishedAfter2022 fourSum) ∨
  (¬ StarredOver1000 fourSum ∧ ¬ PublishedAfter2022 fourSum)

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : hypothesis := by
  have h_cases := premise7
  have h_neither : ¬ StarredOver1000 fourSum ∧ ¬ PublishedAfter2022 fourSum := by
    cases h_cases with

        have : False := h_not_star h1.1

axiom DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood : Entity → Prop
axiom MorallyPerfect : Entity → Prop
axiom God : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Philosopher x → Reasons x
axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Entity, Sophist x ∧ Reasons x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Reasons x → DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood x → ¬ MorallyPerfect x
axiom premise5 : MorallyPerfect God

def Hypothesis : Prop := Sophist God ∧ Philosopher God

theorem reasoning_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have hGodPerfect : MorallyPerfect God := premise5
  have hNotDistinguish : ¬ DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood God := by
    intro hDist
    have hNotPerfect : ¬ MorallyPerfect God := premise4 God hDist
    exact hNotPerfect hGodPerfect
  have hNotReason : ¬ Reasons God := by
    intro hReason
    have hDist : DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood God := premise3 God hReason
    exact hNotDistinguish hDist
  have hPhil : Philosopher God := h.right
  have hReasonGod : Reasons God := premise1 God hPhil
  exact hNotReason hReasonGod

premises:
All philosophers reason. 
Some sophists reason. 
All who can reason can distinguish truth from falsehood.
Nobody who can distinguish truth from falsehood is morally perfect.  
The theistic God is morally perfect.
hypothesis: if the theistic God is a philosopher, then he is not a sophist.
