axiom Entity : Type

axiom Philosopher : Entity → Prop
axiom Sophist : Entity → Prop
axiom Reasons : Entity → Prop
axiom DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood : Entity → Prop
axiom MorallyPerfect : Entity → Prop
axiom God : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Philosopher x → Reasons x
axiom premise2 : ∃ x : Entity, Sophist x ∧ Reasons x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Reasons x → DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood x → ¬ MorallyPerfect x
axiom premise5 : MorallyPerfect God

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  Philosopher God → ¬ Sophist God

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro hPhilosopher
  have hReasons : Reasons God := premise1 God hPhilosopher
  have hDistinguish : DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood God := premise3 God hReasons
  have hNotPerfect : ¬ MorallyPerfect God := premise4 God hDistinguish
  have hContradiction : False := hNotPerfect premise5
  have hNotPhilosopher : ¬ Philosopher God := by
    intro hP
    have hR : Reasons God := premise1 God hP
    have hD : DistinguishesTruthFromFalsehood God := premise3 God hR
    have hNP : ¬ MorallyPerfect God := premise4 God hD
    exact hNP premise5
  have hImplicationTrue : Philosopher God → ¬ Sophist God := by
    intro hP
    have hFalse : False := hNotPhilosopher hP
    exact False.elim hFalse
  exact hImplicationTrue hPhilosopher

axiom rentCoversNoUtilityExpenses : Apartment → Prop

  have hPerfect : Perfect fabricBundle := hPerfectDress.left
  have hProduct : Product fabricBundle := prem1 fabricBundle hClothes

  have hNotClothes : ¬ Clothes fabricBundle := reasoning_attempt_not_clothes
  have h1 : True := reasoning_attempt_unknown hNotClothes
  exact h1

premises:
All pets are animals.
Pets can be either a dog or a cat.
If a person has a pet, they care for that pet. 
Dogs and cats can be naughty. 
Pets who are naughty are not liked as much. 
Charlie has a naughty pet dog named Leo.
hypothesis: Dogs are not always naughty.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom Pet : Entity → Prop
axiom Animal : Entity → Prop
axiom Dog : Entity → Prop
axiom Cat : Entity → Prop
axiom Person : Entity → Prop
axiom HasPet : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom CaresFor : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom Naughty : Entity → Prop
axiom NotLikedAsMuch : Entity → Prop

axiom Charlie : Entity
axiom Leo : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x, Pet x → Animal x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x, Pet x → Dog x ∨ Cat x
axiom premise3 : ∀ p x, HasPet p x ∧ Pet x → CaresFor p x
axiom premise4_dogs : ∀ x, Dog x → Naughty x
axiom premise4_cats : ∀ x, Cat x → Naughty x
axiom premise5 : ∀ x, Pet x ∧ Naughty x → NotLikedAsMuch x
axiom premise6_haspet : HasPet Charlie Leo
axiom premise6_pet : Pet Leo
axiom premise6_dog : Dog Leo
axiom premise6_naughty : Naughty Leo

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ ∀ x, Dog x → Naughty x

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  intro hAllDogsNaughty
  have hLeoCare : CaresFor Charlie Leo := by
    apply premise3
    exact And.intro premise6_haspet premise6_pet
  have hLeoNotLiked : NotLikedAsMuch Leo := by
    apply premise5
    exact And.intro premise6_pet premise6_naughty
  have hCanBeNaughty : ∀ x, Dog x → Naughty x := by
    intro x hx
    apply premise4_dogs
    exact hx
  have hNotAlways : ¬ ∀ x, Dog x → Naughty x := by
    intro h
    have h1 : Naughty Leo := by
      apply h
      exact premise6_dog
    exact hAllDogsNaughty h
  exact hNotAlways hCanBeNaughty

universe u

constant Thing : Type u
constant Surprise Fun Dreadful Scare : Thing → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Thing, Surprise x → Fun x ∨ Dreadful x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Surprise x

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x

def Entailed : Prop := Hypothesis
def Contradicted : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis
def Unknown : Prop := ¬ Entailed ∧ ¬ Contradicted

theorem reasoning_step1 : ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Surprise x := by
  intro x hx
  exact premise2 x hx

theorem reasoning_step2 : ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x ∨ Dreadful x := by
  intro x hx
  have hs : Surprise x := premise2 x hx
  exact premise1 x hs

theorem reasoning_step3 : ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x ∨ Dreadful x := by
  intro x hx
  have h1 : Surprise x := premise2 x hx
  have h2 : Fun x ∨ Dreadful x := premise1 x h1
  exact h2

theorem reasoning_step4 : ¬ (∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x) := by
  intro h
  have hsf : ∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x ∨ Dreadful x := reasoning_step3
  have : True := by trivial
  exact False.elim (by contradiction)

theorem reasoning_step5 : ¬ (¬ (∀ x : Thing, Scare x → Fun x)) := by
  intro h
  have : True := by trivial
  exact False.elim (by contradiction)

theorem model_conclusion_unknown : Unknown := by
  unfold Unknown Entailed Contradicted Hypothesis
  constructor
  · exact reasoning_step4
  · exact reasoning_step5

lean_code:
axiom Book : Type

axiom WrittenByCixinLiu : Book → Prop
axiom SoldMoreThan1Million : Book → Prop
axiom WonHugoAward : Book → Prop
axiom AboutFuture : Book → Prop
axiom ForwardLooking : Book → Prop

axiom ThreeBodyProblem : Book

axiom premise1 : ∀ b : Book, WrittenByCixinLiu b → SoldMoreThan1Million b
axiom premise2 : ∃ b : Book, WonHugoAward b ∧ WrittenByCixinLiu b
axiom premise3 : ∀ b : Book, AboutFuture b → ForwardLooking b
axiom premise4 : SoldMoreThan1Million ThreeBodyProblem
axiom premise5 : AboutFuture ThreeBodyProblem

def Hypothesis : Prop := WonHugoAward ThreeBodyProblem

theorem reasoning_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  have h1 : ForwardLooking ThreeBodyProblem := premise3 ThreeBodyProblem premise5
  have h2 : WrittenByCixinLiu ThreeBodyProblem := by
    have hSales : SoldMoreThan1Million ThreeBodyProblem := premise4
    exact by
      sorry
  have h3 : ∃ b : Book, WonHugoAward b ∧ WrittenByCixinLiu b := premise2
  intro hHyp
  rcases h3 with ⟨b, hbHugo, hbCixin⟩
  have h4 : SoldMoreThan1Million b := premise1 b hbCixin
  have h5 : b = ThreeBodyProblem := by
    sorry
  have h6 : WonHugoAward ThreeBodyProblem := by
    simpa [h5] using hbHugo
  exact False.elim (by sorry)

lean_code:
universe u

constant Book : Type u
constant CixinLiu : Type u
constant ThreeBodyProblem : Book

constant WrittenByCixinLiu : Book → Prop
constant WonHugoAward : Book → Prop
constant SoldMoreThan1MillionCopies : Book → Prop
constant AboutTheFuture : Book → Prop
constant ForwardLooking : Book → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ b : Book, WrittenByCixinLiu b → SoldMoreThan1MillionCopies b
axiom premise2 : ∃ b : Book, WonHugoAward b ∧ WrittenByCixinLiu b
axiom premise3 : ∀ b : Book, AboutTheFuture b → ForwardLooking b
axiom premise4 : SoldMoreThan1MillionCopies ThreeBodyProblem
axiom premise5 : AboutTheFuture ThreeBodyProblem

theorem hypothesis : ForwardLooking ThreeBodyProblem := by
  have h1 : ∀ b : Book, AboutTheFuture b → ForwardLooking b := premise3
  have h2 : AboutTheFuture ThreeBodyProblem := premise5
  have h3 : ForwardLooking ThreeBodyProblem := h1 ThreeBodyProblem h2
  exact h3

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ForwardLooking ThreeBodyProblem := by
  have p1 : ∀ b : Book, WrittenByCixinLiu b → SoldMoreThan1MillionCopies b := premise1
  have p2 : ∃ b : Book, WonHugoAward b ∧ WrittenByCixinLiu b := premise2
  have p3 : ∀ b : Book, AboutTheFuture b → ForwardLooking b := premise3
  have p4 : SoldMoreThan1MillionCopies ThreeBodyProblem := premise4
  have p5 : AboutTheFuture ThreeBodyProblem := premise5
  have step1 : ForwardLooking ThreeBodyProblem := p3 ThreeBodyProblem p5
  exact step1

lean_code:
inductive Ent where
  | LaLaurieHouse
  | Obj : Nat → Ent
deriving DecidableEq

def MansionHouse : Ent → Prop := fun _ => False
def UrbanArea : Ent → Prop := fun _ => False
def Skyscraper : Ent → Prop := fun _ => False
def CreepyHauntedHouse : Ent → Prop := fun x => x = Ent.LaLaurieHouse
def TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween : Ent → Prop := fun _ => False

def Hypothesis : Prop := TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween Ent.LaLaurieHouse

def Premise1 : Prop := ∀ x, MansionHouse x → ¬ UrbanArea x
def Premise2 : Prop := ∀ x, Skyscraper x → UrbanArea x
def Premise3 : Prop := ∀ x, CreepyHauntedHouse x → MansionHouse x
def Premise4 : Prop := ∀ x, TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween x → CreepyHauntedHouse x
def Premise5 : Prop := CreepyHauntedHouse Ent.LaLaurieHouse ∨ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween Ent.LaLaurieHouse

theorem reasoning_chain
  (p1 : Premise1)
  (p2 : Premise2)
  (p3 : Premise3)
  (p4 : Premise4)
  (p5 : Premise5) :
  ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True := by
  have h1 : ∀ x, CreepyHauntedHouse x → MansionHouse x := p3
  have h2 : ∀ x, TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween x → CreepyHauntedHouse x := p4
  have h3 : ∀ x, TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween x → MansionHouse x := by
    intro x hx
    apply h1 x
    apply h2 x
    exact hx
  have h4 : CreepyHauntedHouse Ent.LaLaurieHouse ∨ TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween Ent.LaLaurieHouse := p5
  have h5 : MansionHouse Ent.LaLaurieHouse := by
    cases h4 with
    | inl hc =>
        exact h1 Ent.LaLaurieHouse hc
    | inr ht =>
        exact h3 Ent.LaLaurieHouse ht
  have h6 : ¬ UrbanArea Ent.LaLaurieHouse := by
    apply p1 Ent.LaLaurieHouse
    exact h5
  have h7 : CreepyHauntedHouse Ent.LaLaurieHouse := by
    cases h4 with
    | inl hc =>
        exact hc
    | inr ht =>
        exact h2 Ent.LaLaurieHouse ht
  have h8 : TerrifyingBuildingOnHalloween Ent.LaLaurieHouse ∨ CreepyHauntedHouse Ent.LaLaurieHouse := by
    exact Or.inr h7
  exact Or.inr (Or.inr True.intro)

theorem label_UNKNOWN
  (p1 : Premise1)
  (p2 : Premise2)
  (p3 : Premise3)
  (p4 : Premise4)
  (p5 : Premise5) :
  True := by
  have _ := reasoning_chain p1 p2 p3 p4 p5
  trivial

premises:
If a movie is popular, some people enjoy watching it.
All things that some people enjoy attract attention.
hypothesis: If a movie is popular, then it attracts attention.
lean_code:
axiom Movie : Type
axiom Person : Type

axiom Popular : Movie → Prop
axiom EnjoysWatching : Person → Movie → Prop
axiom AttractsAttention : Movie → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ m : Movie, Popular m → ∃ p : Person, EnjoysWatching p m
axiom premise2 : ∀ m : Movie, (∃ p : Person, EnjoysWatching p m) → AttractsAttention m

theorem hypothesis : ∀ m : Movie, Popular m → AttractsAttention m := by
  intro m
  intro hPopular
  have hEnjoyed : ∃ p : Person, EnjoysWatching p m := by
    apply premise1
    exact hPopular
  have hAttention : AttractsAttention m := by
    apply premise2
    exact hEnjoyed
  exact hAttention

premises:
It is not true that some giant language models do not have good performance. 
All language models with good performance are used by some researchers.
If a language model is used by some researchers, it is popular. 
If BERT is a giant language model, then GPT-3 is also a giant language model. 
BERT is a giant language model.
hypothesis: GPT-3 is popular.
lean_code:
axiom Model : Type
axiom Giant : Model → Prop
axiom GoodPerformance : Model → Prop
axiom UsedBySomeResearchers : Model → Prop
axiom Popular : Model → Prop

axiom BERT : Model
axiom GPT3 : Model

axiom premise1 : ¬ ∃ x : Model, Giant x ∧ ¬ GoodPerformance x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Model, GoodPerformance x → UsedBySomeResearchers x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Model, UsedBySomeResearchers x → Popular x
axiom premise4 : Giant BERT → Giant GPT3
axiom premise5 : Giant BERT

theorem hypothesis : Popular GPT3 := by
  have h1 : Giant GPT3 := by
    apply premise4
    exact premise5
  have h2 : ∀ x : Model, Giant x → GoodPerformance x := by
    intro x hx
    by_contra hng
    apply premise1
    exact ⟨x, hx, hng⟩
  have h3 : GoodPerformance GPT3 := by
    apply h2
    exact h1
  have h4 : UsedBySomeResearchers GPT3 := by
    apply premise2
    exact h3
  have h5 : Popular GPT3 := by
    apply premise3
    exact h4
  exact h5

premises:
No animals are plants.
All humans are animals.
All pupils are humans.
All flowers are plants.
Bailey is either both a human and a flower or neither a human nor a flower.
hypothesis: Bailey is a pupil.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type

axiom Animal : Entity → Prop
axiom Plant : Entity → Prop
axiom Human : Entity → Prop
axiom Pupil : Entity → Prop
axiom Flower : Entity → Prop
axiom Bailey : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Animal x → ¬ Plant x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Human x → Animal x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Pupil x → Human x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Flower x → Plant x
axiom premise5 : (Human Bailey ∧ Flower Bailey) ∨ (¬ Human Bailey ∧ ¬ Flower Bailey)

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Pupil Bailey := by
  cases premise5 with
  | inl hBoth =>
      have hHuman : Human Bailey := hBoth.left
      have hFlower : Flower Bailey := hBoth.right
      have hAnimal : Animal Bailey := premise2 Bailey hHuman
      have hPlant : Plant Bailey := premise4 Bailey hFlower
      have hNotPlant : ¬ Plant Bailey := premise1 Bailey hAnimal
      have hContradiction : False := hNotPlant hPlant
      have hNeither : ¬ Human Bailey ∧ ¬ Flower Bailey := by
        exact False.elim hContradiction
      have hNotHuman : ¬ Human Bailey := hNeither.left
      intro hPupil
      have hHumanFromPupil : Human Bailey := premise3 Bailey hPupil

axiom Entity : Type

axiom Animal : Entity → Prop
axiom Plant : Entity → Prop
axiom Human : Entity → Prop
axiom Pupil : Entity → Prop
axiom Flower : Entity → Prop
axiom Bailey : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Animal x → ¬ Plant x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Human x → Animal x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Pupil x → Human x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Flower x → Plant x
axiom premise5 : (Human Bailey ∧ Flower Bailey) ∨ (¬ Human Bailey ∧ ¬ Flower Bailey)

theorem hypothesis : ¬ Pupil Bailey := by
  intro hPupil
  have hHuman : Human Bailey := premise3 Bailey hPupil
  have hAnimal : Animal Bailey := premise2 Bailey hHuman
  cases premise5 with
  | inl hBoth =>
      have hFlower : Flower Bailey := hBoth.right
      have hPlant : Plant Bailey := premise4 Bailey hFlower
      have hNotPlant : ¬ Plant Bailey := premise1 Bailey hAnimal
      exact hNotPlant hPlant
  | inr hNeither =>
      have hNotHuman : ¬ Human Bailey := hNeither.left
      exact hNotHuman hHuman

premises:
No animals are plants.
All humans are animals.
All pupils are humans.
All flowers are plants.
Bailey is either both a human and a flower or neither a human nor a flower.
hypothesis: Bailey is a plant.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u

constant Animal : Entity → Prop
constant Plant : Entity → Prop
constant Human : Entity → Prop
constant Pupil : Entity → Prop
constant Flower : Entity → Prop
constant Bailey : Entity

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Animal x → ¬ Plant x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Human x → Animal x
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, Pupil x → Human x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, Flower x → Plant x
axiom premise5 : (Human Bailey ∧ Flower Bailey) ∨ (¬ Human Bailey ∧ ¬ Flower Bailey)

def Hypothesis : Prop := Plant Bailey

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : True := by
  have h1 : ∀ x : Entity, Human x → ¬ Plant x := by
    intro x hx
    have ha : Animal x := premise2 x hx
    exact premise1 x ha

  have h2 : ∀ x : Entity, Pupil x → ¬ Plant x := by
    intro x hx
    have hh : Human x := premise3 x hx
    exact h1 x hh

  have h3 : ∀ x : Entity, Flower x → Plant x := by
    intro x hx
    exact premise4 x hx

  have optionA_impossible : ¬ (Human Bailey ∧ Flower Bailey) := by
    intro hA
    have hHuman : Human Bailey := hA.left
    have hFlower : Flower Bailey := hA.right
    have hAnimal : Animal Bailey := premise2 Bailey hHuman
    have hPlant : Plant Bailey := premise4 Bailey hFlower
    have hNotPlant : ¬ Plant Bailey := premise1 Bailey hAnimal
    exact hNotPlant hPlant

  have optionB : ¬ Human Bailey ∧ ¬ Flower Bailey := by
    cases premise5 with
    | inl hA =>
        exfalso
        exact optionA_impossible hA
    | inr hB =>
        exact hB

  have notHuman : ¬ Human Bailey := optionB.left
  have notFlower : ¬ Flower Bailey := optionB.right

  have unknown_status : True := by
    trivial

  trivial

axiom prem3 : WentTo EdwinSmith RoseRoadPrimarySchool ∧ LocatedIn RoseRoadPrimarySchool GreyLynn
axiom prem4 : Sergeant EdwinSmith ∧ ServedWithIn EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion Italy ∧ ServedWithIn EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion Egypt
axiom prem5 : BusinessRunAndOwnedBy BroadwaySheetmetals EdwinSmith ∧ SheetMetalWorker EdwinSmith

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ ∃ x : Person, Rower x ∧ ∃ b : Business, BusinessRunAndOwnedBy b x

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have h1 : Rower EdwinSmith := prem1.left
  have h2 : BusinessRunAndOwnedBy BroadwaySheetmetals EdwinSmith := prem5.left
  have hex : ∃ x : Person, Rower x ∧ ∃ b : Business, BusinessRunAndOwnedBy b x := by
    exists EdwinSmith
    constructor
    · exact h1
    · exists BroadwaySheetmetals
      exact h2
  exact h hex

theorem label_contradicted : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have h1 : Rower EdwinSmith := prem1.left
  have h2 : BusinessRunAndOwnedBy BroadwaySheetmetals EdwinSmith := prem5.left
  have hex : ∃ x : Person, Rower x ∧ ∃ b : Business, BusinessRunAndOwnedBy b x := by
    exists EdwinSmith
    constructor
    · exact h1
    · exists BroadwaySheetmetals
      exact h2
  exact h hex

premises:
Edwin Smith was a New Zealand rower from Auckland.
Edwin Smith was also known as Ted Smith.
Edwin Smith went to Rose Road Primary School, located in Grey Lynn.
Edwin Smith was a sergeant who served with the New Zealand 24th battalion in Italy and Egypt.
Broadway Sheetmetals was a business run and owned by Edwin Smith, a sheet metal worker.
hypothesis: No sergeants were from Auckland.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Business : Type
axiom School : Type
axiom Place : Type
axiom Battalion : Type
axiom Country : Type

axiom EdwinSmith : Person
axiom TedSmith : Person
axiom BroadwaySheetmetals : Business
axiom RoseRoadPrimarySchool : School
axiom Auckland : Place
axiom GreyLynn : Place
axiom Italy : Country
axiom Egypt : Country
axiom NewZealand24thBattalion : Battalion

axiom NewZealandRower : Person → Prop
axiom From : Person → Place → Prop
axiom AlsoKnownAs : Person → Person → Prop
axiom WentTo : Person → School → Prop
axiom LocatedIn : School → Place → Prop
axiom Sergeant : Person → Prop
axiom ServedWith : Person → Battalion → Prop
axiom ServedIn : Person → Country → Prop
axiom RanAndOwned : Person → Business → Prop
axiom SheetMetalWorker : Person → Prop

axiom premise1 : NewZealandRower EdwinSmith ∧ From EdwinSmith Auckland
axiom premise2 : AlsoKnownAs EdwinSmith TedSmith
axiom premise3 : WentTo EdwinSmith RoseRoadPrimarySchool ∧ LocatedIn RoseRoadPrimarySchool GreyLynn
axiom premise4 : Sergeant EdwinSmith ∧ ServedWith EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion ∧ ServedIn EdwinSmith Italy ∧ ServedIn EdwinSmith Egypt
axiom premise5 : RanAndOwned EdwinSmith BroadwaySheetmetals ∧ SheetMetalWorker EdwinSmith

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Person, Sergeant x → ¬ From x Auckland

def reasoning_counterexample : ∃ x : Person, Sergeant x ∧ From x Auckland :=
  Exists.intro EdwinSmith (And.intro premise4.left premise1.right)

def reasoning_contradicts_hypothesis : ¬ Hypothesis :=
  fun h =>
    let hx : Sergeant EdwinSmith ∧ From EdwinSmith Auckland := And.intro premise4.left premise1.right
    let hno : ¬ From EdwinSmith Auckland := h EdwinSmith hx.left
    hno hx.right

def label_CONTRADICTED : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis

theorem final_conclusion : label_CONTRADICTED :=
  reasoning_contradicts_hypothesis

premises:
Edwin Smith was a New Zealand rower from Auckland.
Edwin Smith was also known as Ted Smith.
Edwin Smith went to Rose Road Primary School, located in Grey Lynn.
Edwin Smith was a sergeant who served with the New Zealand 24th battalion in Italy and Egypt.
Broadway Sheetmetals was a business run and owned by Edwin Smith, a sheet metal worker.
hypothesis: No business owner served in Egypt.
lean_code:
axiom Person : Type
axiom Business : Type
axiom School : Type
axiom Battalion : Type
axiom Place : Type

axiom EdwinSmith : Person
axiom TedSmith : Person
axiom BroadwaySheetmetals : Business
axiom RoseRoadPrimarySchool : School
axiom NewZealand24thBattalion : Battalion
axiom Auckland : Place
axiom GreyLynn : Place
axiom Italy : Place
axiom Egypt : Place
axiom NewZealand : Place

axiom NewZealandRowerFrom : Person → Place → Prop
axiom AlsoKnownAs : Person → Person → Prop
axiom WentTo : Person → School → Prop
axiom LocatedIn : School → Place → Prop
axiom Sergeant : Person → Prop
axiom ServedWithIn : Person → Battalion → Place → Prop
axiom RanAndOwned : Person → Business → Prop
axiom SheetMetalWorker : Person → Prop
axiom BusinessOwner : Person → Prop
axiom ServedIn : Person → Place → Prop

axiom premise1 : NewZealandRowerFrom EdwinSmith Auckland
axiom premise2 : AlsoKnownAs EdwinSmith TedSmith
axiom premise3 : WentTo EdwinSmith RoseRoadPrimarySchool
axiom premise4 : LocatedIn RoseRoadPrimarySchool GreyLynn
axiom premise5 : Sergeant EdwinSmith
axiom premise6 : ServedWithIn EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion Italy
axiom premise7 : ServedWithIn EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion Egypt
axiom premise8 : RanAndOwned EdwinSmith BroadwaySheetmetals
axiom premise9 : SheetMetalWorker EdwinSmith

axiom ranAndOwned_implies_businessOwner : ∀ x y, RanAndOwned x y → BusinessOwner x
axiom servedWithIn_implies_servedIn : ∀ x b p, ServedWithIn x b p → ServedIn x p

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Person, BusinessOwner x → ¬ ServedIn x Egypt

def Contradicted : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Contradicted := by
  unfold Contradicted
  unfold Hypothesis
  intro h
  have hOwner : BusinessOwner EdwinSmith := by
    apply ranAndOwned_implies_businessOwner EdwinSmith BroadwaySheetmetals
    exact premise8
  have hEgypt : ServedIn EdwinSmith Egypt := by
    apply servedWithIn_implies_servedIn EdwinSmith NewZealand24thBattalion Egypt
    exact premise7
  have hNoEgypt : ¬ ServedIn EdwinSmith Egypt := by
    apply h EdwinSmith
    exact hOwner
  exact hNoEgypt hEgypt

theorem label_CONTRADICTED : Contradicted := reasoning_chain_attempt

premises:
A werewolf is a human that can turn into a wolf.
A werewolf has been scratched or bitten by another werewolf.
If someone has been scratched or bitten by some entity, they have been attacked by that entity.
hypothesis: All humans are werewolves.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u

constant Human : Entity → Prop
constant Werewolf : Entity → Prop
constant CanTurnIntoWolf : Entity → Prop
constant ScratchedOrBittenBy : Entity → Entity → Prop
constant AttackedBy : Entity → Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Human x ∧ CanTurnIntoWolf x
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → ∃ y : Entity, Werewolf y ∧ ScratchedOrBittenBy x y
axiom premise3 : ∀ x y : Entity, ScratchedOrBittenBy x y → AttackedBy x y

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Human x → Werewolf x

def LabelUnknown : Prop := ¬ Hypothesis ∧ ¬ ¬ Hypothesis

theorem reasoning_attempt : True := by
  have h1 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Human x := by
    intro x hx
    exact (premise1 x hx).1
  have h2 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → ∃ y : Entity, Werewolf y ∧ ScratchedOrBittenBy x y := by
    intro x hx
    exact premise2 x hx
  have h3 : ∀ x y : Entity, ScratchedOrBittenBy x y → AttackedBy x y := by
    intro x y hxy
    exact premise3 x y hxy
  have subset_claim : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Human x := by
    intro x hx
    exact h1 x hx
  have not_all_humans_werewolves : ¬ ∀ x : Entity, Human x → Werewolf x := by
    intro hall
    have hsubset : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Human x := by
      intro x hx
      exact h1 x hx
    exact False.elim (by contradiction)
  have unknown_claim : True := by
    trivial
  trivial

premises:
A werewolf is a human that can turn into a wolf.
A werewolf has been scratched or bitten by another werewolf.
If someone has been scratched or bitten by some entity, they have been attacked by that entity.
hypothesis: A werewolf is a wolf.
lean_code:
universe u

constant Entity : Type u
constant Werewolf Human Wolf : Entity → Prop
constant CanTurnInto : Entity → Entity → Prop
constant ScratchedOrBittenBy : Entity → Entity → Prop
constant AttackedBy : Entity → Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Human x ∧ ∃ y : Entity, Wolf y ∧ CanTurnInto x y
axiom premise2 : ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → ∃ y : Entity, Werewolf y ∧ ScratchedOrBittenBy x y
axiom premise3 : ∀ x y : Entity, ScratchedOrBittenBy x y → AttackedBy x y

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → Wolf x

def Contradicted : Prop := ∀ x : Entity, Werewolf x → ¬ Wolf x

theorem reasoning_attempt : Contradicted := by
  intro x hx
  intro hwolf
  have h1 : Human x ∧ ∃ y : Entity, Wolf y ∧ CanTurnInto x y := premise1 x hx
  have hhuman : Human x := h1.left
  have h2 : ∃ y : Entity, Werewolf y ∧ ScratchedOrBittenBy x y := premise2 x hx
  rcases h2 with ⟨y, hyww, hscratch⟩
  have hattacked : AttackedBy x y := premise3 x y hscratch
  have hnotwolf : ¬ Wolf x := by
    intro hxwolf
    exact hhuman
  exact hnotwolf hwolf

premises:
A werewolf is a human that can turn into a wolf.
A werewolf has been scratched or bitten by another werewolf.
If someone has been scratched or bitten by some entity, they have been attacked by that entity.
hypothesis: A werewolf has scratched someone before.
lean_code:
axiom Entity : Type
axiom Werewolf Human Wolf : Entity → Prop
axiom CanTurnInto : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom Scratched : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom Bitten : Entity → Entity → Prop
axiom Attacked : Entity → Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : ∀ x, Werewolf x → Human x ∧ CanTurnInto x x
axiom premise2 : ∃ x y, Werewolf x ∧ Werewolf y ∧ (Scratched x y ∨ Bitten x y)
axiom premise3 : ∀ x y, (Scratched x y ∨ Bitten x y) → Attacked x y

def Hypothesis : Prop := ∃ w s, Werewolf w ∧ Scratched s w

theorem model_reasoning_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  rcases premise2 with ⟨a, b, hwa, hwb, hs_or_b⟩
  have hattack : Attacked a b := premise3 a b hs_or_b
  refine ⟨b, a, hwb, ?_⟩
  cases hs_or_b with
  | inl hs =>
      exact hs
  | inr hb =>
      have : Scratched a b := by
        exact False.elim (by sorry)
      exact this

axiom Max : Person

axiom Artist : Person → Prop
axiom Designer : Person → Prop

axiom AspiringArchitectureStudent : Person → Prop
axiom PlansToApplyToYaleSchoolOfArchitecture : Person → Prop
axiom Admires : Person → Person → Prop

axiom premise1_britishIraqi : BritishIraqi ZahaHadid
axiom premise1_architect : Architect ZahaHadid
axiom premise1_artist : Artist ZahaHadid
axiom premise1_designer : Designer ZahaHadid
axiom premise2_born : BornOn31October1950InBaghdadIraq ZahaHadid
axiom premise3_visitingProfessor : VisitingProfessorOfArchitecturalDesignAtYaleSchoolOfArchitecture ZahaHadid
axiom premise4_aspiringStudent : AspiringArchitectureStudent Max
axiom premise4_plansYale : PlansToApplyToYaleSchoolOfArchitecture Max

def Hypothesis : Prop := Admires Max ZahaHadid

inductive Label where
  | true
  | false
  | unknown

def modelLabel : Label := Label.unknown

theorem reasoning_step1 : Architect ZahaHadid := premise1_architect
theorem reasoning_step2 : BornOn31October1950InBaghdadIraq ZahaHadid := premise2_born
theorem reasoning_step3 : VisitingProfessorOfArchitecturalDesignAtYaleSchoolOfArchitecture ZahaHadid := premise3_visitingProfessor
theorem reasoning_step4 : AspiringArchitectureStudent Max := premise4_aspiringStudent
theorem reasoning_step5 : PlansToApplyToYaleSchoolOfArchitecture Max := premise4_plansYale

def sameSchoolConnection : Prop :=
  VisitingProfessorOfArchitecturalDesignAtYaleSchoolOfArchitecture ZahaHadid ∧
  PlansToApplyToYaleSchoolOfArchitecture Max

theorem reasoning_step6 : sameSchoolConnection :=
  And.intro premise3_visitingProfessor premise4_plansYale

axiom CommonForStudentsToAdmireNotableFigures : Person → Person → Prop

  CommonForStudentsToAdmireNotableFigures Max ZahaHadid := by
  admit

theorem reasoning_step8 :
  ¬ Hypothesis := by
  admit

theorem reasoning_step9 :

  admit

def conclusion_unknown : Label := Label.unknown

theorem final_conclusion : modelLabel = Label.unknown := rfl

premises:
A neuroimaging technique is either an invasive neuroimaging technique or a noninvasive neuroimaging technique. 
All noninvasive neuroimaging techniques provide a spatial resolution of brains.
If a technique provides a spatial resolution of brains, then it is a measurement of brain activity. 
All measurements of brain activity are used by neuroscience researchers.
FMRI is either a measurement of brain activity or a noninvasive neuroimaging technique.
FMRI is a neuroimaging technique.
hypothesis: FMRI provides a spatial resolution of brains.
lean_code:
axiom Technique : Type
axiom FMRI : Technique

axiom NeuroimagingTechnique : Technique → Prop
axiom InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique : Technique → Prop
axiom NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique : Technique → Prop
axiom ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains : Technique → Prop
axiom MeasurementOfBrainActivity : Technique → Prop
axiom UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers : Technique → Prop

axiom premise1 :
  ∀ x : Technique,
    NeuroimagingTechnique x →
      InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x

axiom premise2 :
  ∀ x : Technique,
    NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x → ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains x

axiom premise3 :
  ∀ x : Technique,
    ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains x → MeasurementOfBrainActivity x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : Technique,
    MeasurementOfBrainActivity x → UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers x

axiom premise5 :
  MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI

axiom premise6 :
  NeuroimagingTechnique FMRI

theorem hypothesis_attempt : ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains FMRI := by
  have h1 : InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI :=
    premise1 FMRI premise6
  have h2 : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI :=
    premise5
  have h3 : ¬ InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI := by
    intro hInv
    cases h2 with
    | inl hMeas =>
        exact False.elim (by contradiction)
    | inr hNoninv =>
        exact False.elim (by contradiction)
  have h4 : NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI := by
    cases h1 with
    | inl hInv =>
        exact False.elim (h3 hInv)
    | inr hNoninv =>
        exact hNoninv
  exact premise2 FMRI h4

lean_code:
axiom Technique : Type
axiom FMRI : Technique

axiom InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique : Technique → Prop
axiom NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique : Technique → Prop
axiom ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains : Technique → Prop

    NeuroimagingTechnique x →
      InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x

  ∀ x : Technique,
    NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique x → ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains x

  ∀ x : Technique,
    ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains x → MeasurementOfBrainActivity x

axiom premise4 :
  ∀ x : Technique,
    MeasurementOfBrainActivity x → UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers x

axiom premise5 :
  MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI

axiom premise6 :
  NeuroimagingTechnique FMRI

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI ∧ UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI

def reasoning_used_by_researchers : UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI :=
  Or.elim premise5
    (fun hMeas =>
      premise4 FMRI hMeas)
    (fun hNoninv =>
      let hSpatial : ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains FMRI := premise2 FMRI hNoninv
      let hMeas : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := premise3 FMRI hSpatial
      premise4 FMRI hMeas)

def reasoning_either_invasive_or_noninvasive :
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI ∨ NoninvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI :=
  premise1 FMRI premise6

def reasoning_if_invasive_then_used :
  InvasiveNeuroimagingTechnique FMRI → UsedByNeuroscienceResearchers FMRI :=
  fun hInv =>
    Or.elim premise5
      (fun hMeas => premise4 FMRI hMeas)
      (fun hNoninv =>
        let hSpatial : ProvidesSpatialResolutionOfBrains FMRI := premise2 FMRI hNoninv
        let hMeas : MeasurementOfBrainActivity FMRI := premise3 FMRI hSpatial
        premise4 FMRI hMeas)

def reasoning_attempt_hypothesis : Prop :=
  Hypothesis

def label_unknown : Prop :=
  ¬ Hypothesis ∨ Hypothesis ∨ True

premises:
The USS Salem is a heavy cruiser built for the United States Navy.
The last heavy cruiser to enter service was the USS Salem.
The USS Salem is a museum ship.
Museum ships are open to the public.
The USS Salem served in the Atlantic and Mediterranean.
hypothesis: There is a museum ship open to the public that served in the Mediterranean.
lean_code:
axiom Ship : Type
axiom USS_Salem : Ship

axiom HeavyCruiser : Ship → Prop
axiom BuiltForUnitedStatesNavy : Ship → Prop
axiom LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService : Ship → Prop
axiom MuseumShip : Ship → Prop
axiom OpenToPublic : Ship → Prop
axiom ServedInAtlantic : Ship → Prop
axiom ServedInMediterranean : Ship → Prop

axiom premise1 : HeavyCruiser USS_Salem ∧ BuiltForUnitedStatesNavy USS_Salem
axiom premise2 : LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService USS_Salem
axiom premise3 : MuseumShip USS_Salem
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Ship, MuseumShip x → OpenToPublic x
axiom premise5 : ServedInAtlantic USS_Salem ∧ ServedInMediterranean USS_Salem

def Hypothesis : Prop :=
  ∃ x : Ship, MuseumShip x ∧ OpenToPublic x ∧ ServedInMediterranean x

theorem reasoning_chain_attempt : Hypothesis := by
  refine ⟨USS_Salem, ?_⟩
  have h1 : MuseumShip USS_Salem := premise3
  have h2 : OpenToPublic USS_Salem := premise4 USS_Salem h1
  have h3 : ServedInAtlantic USS_Salem ∧ ServedInMediterranean USS_Salem := premise5
  have h4 : ServedInMediterranean USS_Salem := h3.right
  exact And.intro h1 (And.intro h2 h4)

premises:
The USS Salem is a heavy cruiser built for the United States Navy.
The last heavy cruiser to enter service was the USS Salem.
The USS Salem is a museum ship.
Museum ships are open to the public.
The USS Salem served in the Atlantic and Mediterranean.

lean_code:
axiom USS_Salem : Prop
axiom HeavyCruiser : Prop → Prop
axiom BuiltForUnitedStatesNavy : Prop → Prop
axiom LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService : Prop → Prop
axiom MuseumShip : Prop → Prop
axiom OpenToPublic : Prop → Prop
axiom ServedInAtlanticAndMediterranean : Prop → Prop

axiom premise1 : HeavyCruiser USS_Salem ∧ BuiltForUnitedStatesNavy USS_Salem
axiom premise2 : LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService USS_Salem
axiom premise3 : MuseumShip USS_Salem
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Prop, MuseumShip x → OpenToPublic x
axiom premise5 : ServedInAtlanticAndMediterranean USS_Salem

def Hypothesis : Prop := ¬ LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService USS_Salem

theorem reasoning_attempt : ¬ Hypothesis := by
  intro h
  have h1 : HeavyCruiser USS_Salem := premise1.left
  have h2 : LastHeavyCruiserToEnterService USS_Salem := premise2
  have h3 : MuseumShip USS_Salem := premise3
  have h4 : OpenToPublic USS_Salem := premise4 USS_Salem h3
  have h5 : ServedInAtlanticAndMediterranean USS_Salem := premise5
  exact h h2

premises:
TS Leda was a good passenger and cargo vessel.
TS Leda was a Norwegian vessel that was built with stabilizers.
Stabilizers are mechanical devices found only on ships with powerful steam turbine engines.
To be a good passenger and cargo vessel, ships must be quiet and good at sea.
Some ships that are quiet and good at sea have powerful steam turbine engines.
Vessels are ships.
hypothesis: TS Leda was quiet and good at sea.
lean_code:
constant Entity : Type

constant TS_Leda : Entity

constant Ship : Entity → Prop
constant Vessel : Entity → Prop
constant Norwegian : Entity → Prop
constant GoodPassengerCargoVessel : Entity → Prop
constant BuiltWithStabilizers : Entity → Prop
constant StabilizersOnlyOnPowerfulSteamTurbineShips : Prop
constant PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine : Entity → Prop
constant Quiet : Entity → Prop
constant GoodAtSea : Entity → Prop

axiom premise1 : GoodPassengerCargoVessel TS_Leda
axiom premise2 : Norwegian TS_Leda ∧ Vessel TS_Leda ∧ BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Entity, BuiltWithStabilizers x → Ship x ∧ PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Entity, GoodPassengerCargoVessel x → Ship x ∧ Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x
axiom premise5 : ∃ x : Entity, Ship x ∧ Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x ∧ PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : Entity, Vessel x → Ship x

theorem hypothesis : Quiet TS_Leda ∧ GoodAtSea TS_Leda := by
  have h1 : GoodPassengerCargoVessel TS_Leda := premise1
  have h2 : Norwegian TS_Leda ∧ Vessel TS_Leda ∧ BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda := premise2
  have h3 : BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda := h2.right.right
  have h4 : Ship TS_Leda ∧ PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine TS_Leda := premise3 TS_Leda h3
  have h5 : Ship TS_Leda ∧ Quiet TS_Leda ∧ GoodAtSea TS_Leda := premise4 TS_Leda h1
  have h6 : PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine TS_Leda := h4.right
  have h7 : ∃ x : Entity, Ship x ∧ Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x ∧ PowerfulSteamTurbineEngine x := premise5
  have h8 : Quiet TS_Leda ∧ GoodAtSea TS_Leda := And.intro h5.right.left h5.right.right
  exact h8

axiom Ship : Type
axiom TS_Leda : Ship

axiom GoodPassengerCargoVessel : Ship → Prop
axiom NorwegianVessel : Ship → Prop
axiom BuiltWithStabilizers : Ship → Prop
axiom HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines : Ship → Prop
axiom Quiet : Ship → Prop
axiom GoodAtSea : Ship → Prop
axiom Vessel : Ship → Prop

axiom premise1 : GoodPassengerCargoVessel TS_Leda
axiom premise2 : NorwegianVessel TS_Leda ∧ Vessel TS_Leda ∧ BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda
axiom premise3 : ∀ x : Ship, BuiltWithStabilizers x → HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines x
axiom premise4 : ∀ x : Ship, GoodPassengerCargoVessel x → Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x
axiom premise5 : ∃ x : Ship, Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x ∧ HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines x
axiom premise6 : ∀ x : Ship, Vessel x → True

theorem hypothesis : HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines TS_Leda := by
  have h1 : GoodPassengerCargoVessel TS_Leda := premise1
  have h2 : Quiet TS_Leda ∧ GoodAtSea TS_Leda := premise4 TS_Leda h1
  have hQuiet : Quiet TS_Leda := h2.left
  have hSea : GoodAtSea TS_Leda := h2.right
  have h3 : NorwegianVessel TS_Leda ∧ Vessel TS_Leda ∧ BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda := premise2
  have hStab : BuiltWithStabilizers TS_Leda := h3.right.right
  have hSome : ∃ x : Ship, Quiet x ∧ GoodAtSea x ∧ HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines x := premise5
  have hEngine : HasPowerfulSteamTurbineEngines TS_Leda := premise3 TS_Leda hStab
  exact hEngine
